The position logo of the logo; valid values are 'center',

        'top-left', 'top-center' and 'top-right'

keys.description: The logo size in percent relative to the screen width

The default target whitepoint in Kelvin for display calibration,

On: Show Chinese/English and Full/Half shape status as a systray icon

keys.description: Enable fullwidth/halfwidth toggle key (Shift+Space)

keys.name: max-chi-symbol-len
keys.description: Maximum Chinese characters in pre-edit buffer, not including inputing Zhuyin symbols.

keys.name: default-use-english-mode

keys.description: Automatically switch to alphanumeric mode after startup. This mode is sutible when using Chewing as the only IM.

keys.name: chi-eng-mode-toggle
keys.description: Toggle Chinese/Alphanumeric mode with key

keys.name: default-english-case
keys.summary:
Default English letter case
(Only effective when Caps Lock is the toggle key)
keys.description:
no control: No default letter case. Not recommend if you use multiple keyboards or synergy
                lowercase: Default to lowercase, press shift for uppercase.
                uppercase: Default to uppercase, press shift for lowercase.

keys.name: sync-caps-lock
keys.description:
Occasionally, the CapsLock status does not match the IM, this option determines how these status be synchronized. Valid values:
                "disable": Do nothing
                "keyboard": IM status follows keyboard status
                "IM": Keyboard status follows IM status

keys.description: Display a simple notification when an input mode change is triggered by user input.

keys.name: plain-zhuyin

In plain Zhuyin mode, automatic candidate selection and related options are disabled or ignored.
                Deprecated option - use conversion-engine instead.

keys.name: conversion-engine
keys.description:
In Simple mode, automatic candidate selection and related options are disabled or ignored.
                In FuzzyChewing mode, tones or even parts of bopomofo can be omitted.

keys.name: show-page-number

keys.description: When true, tracker-extract will wait for tracker-miner-fs to be done crawling before extracting meta-data. This option is useful on constrained environment where it is important to list files as fast as possible and can wait to get meta-data later.

keys.name: colordictionary

Here you can specify which label to use for candidates in the

        the user database is enabled.

keys.name: labelspellcheck
keys.description:
Here you can choose whether a label is used for candidates in

keys.name: flagdictionary
keys.description:
Here you can choose whether flags are used for candidates in
        the lookup table which come from a dictionary.

keys.name: labelbusy
keys.summary: Use label to indicate busy state
keys.description:
Here you can choose whether a label is used to indicate when
        ibus-typing-booster is busy.

keys.name: labelbusystring
keys.summary: Choose label to indicate busy state
keys.description:
Here you can specify which label is used to indicate when
        ibus-typing-booster is busy.

keys.name: inputmodetruesymbol
keys.description:
The symbol displayed when Typing Booster is active
        (transliterating input, suggesting completions, spellchecking, etc.).

keys.name: inputmodefalsesymbol
keys.description:
The symbol displayed when Typing Booster is disabled
        (input is passed through directly without processing).

keys.name: avoidforwardkeyevent
keys.description:
Use a workaround if the forward_key_event() function is broken.
        Enable this option only if absolutely necessary, 
        it can also cause problems, especially when XIM is used.

keys.name: prefercommit
keys.summary: Prefer commits over passing key events through
keys.description:
Instead of passing a key event through, it is often possible
        to do a commit.  And this is usually the best way to do it. It
        avoids several problems like wrong order of events or wrong
        keycodes. But there are some cases where this may cause
        problems, especially in games which expect real key events.

keys.name: arrowkeysreopenpreedit
keys.description:
Whether it is allowed to put a word back into a preedit when
        the cursor moves to the end or the beginning of a word.
        Enabling this option is useful to correct already
        committed words.

keys.name: wordpredictions
keys.description: Whether ibus-typing-booster should show word predictions.

keys.name: emojipredictions
keys.description:
Whether Unicode symbols and emoji should be included in the
        predictions. Emoji are pictographs like ☺♨⛵…. Unicode symbols
        are other symbols like mathematical symbols (∀∑∯…), arrows
        (←↑↔…), currency symbols (€₹₺…), braille patterns (⠥⠩…), and
        many other symbols. These are technically not emoji but
        nevertheless useful symbols.

keys.name: unicodedataall
keys.description:
Whether to load all Unicode characters from UnicodeData.txt
        for the Unicode symbols and emoji predictions. If false, many
        characters which are typically typed directly rather than
        searched for are ommitted to improve performance.

keys.name: offtherecord
keys.description:
While “Off the record” mode is on, learning from user input is
        disabled. If learned user input is available, predictions are
        usually much better than predictions using only
        dictionaries. Therefore, one should use this option
        sparingly. Only if one wants to avoid saving secret user input
        to disk it might make sense to use this option temporarily.

keys.name: recordmode
keys.description:
Selects which kind of input should be recorded in the user
        database: “Everything”: Every typed word is added to the user
        database or its frequency updated if it is already there.
        “Correctly spelled or previously recorded”: A typed word is only
        added to the user database if it is likely to be correctly
        spelled. If a word is already in the user database its
        frequency is updated no matter how it is spelled.  “Correctly
        spelled”: A typed word is only added to the database or its
        frequency updated in the database if it is likely to be
        correctly spelled.  “Nothing”: Do not record any input at all
        in the user database.

keys.name: emojistyle
keys.description:
To select between colorful emoji (emoji style), monochrome
        emoji (text style), or no style preference.

keys.name: emojitriggercharacters
keys.description:
The characters in this list trigger an emoji and Unicode
        symbol lookup even if the option “Unicode symbols and emoji
        predictions” is off.

keys.name: autocommitcharacters
keys.description:
The characters in this list cause the preedit to be committed
        automatically, followed by a space.  For example, if “.” is an
        auto commit character, this saves you typing a space manually
        after the end of a sentence. You can freely edit this list, a
        reasonable value might be “.,;:?!)”. You should not add
        characters to that list which are needed by your input method,
        for example if you use Latin-Pre (t-latn-pre) it would be a
        bad idea to add “.” to that list because it would prevent you
        from typing “.s” to get “ṡ”. You can also disable this feature
        completely by making the list empty (which is the default).

keys.name: tabenable
keys.description:
If this option is on, suggestions are not shown by
        default. Typing Tab is then necessary to show the list of
        suggestions. After a commit the suggestions are hidden again
        until the next Tab key is typed.

keys.name: rememberlastusedpreeditime
keys.summary: Remember last used preedit input method
keys.description:
If more than one input method is used at the same time, one of
        them is used for the preedit.  Which input method is used for
        the preedit can be changed via the menu or via shortcut
        keys. If this option is enabled, such a change is remembered
        even if the session is restarted.

keys.name: inputmode
keys.description: Whether ibus-typing-booster is on or off.

keys.name: rememberinputmode
keys.description:
If this option is enabled, the last used input mode (on/off)
        is remembered even if the session is restarted.

keys.name: disableinterminals
keys.description: Whether ibus-typing-booster should be disabled in terminals.

keys.name: asciidigits
keys.summary: Convert language specific digits to ASCII digits
keys.description:
Some input methods produce language specific digits by
        default. If one never wants to use such language specific
        digits but prefers western (ASCII) digits always, one can set
        this option to convert language specific digits produced
        by input methods always to ASCII digits.

keys.name: addspaceoncommit
keys.summary: Add a space when committing by label or mouse
keys.description:
Add a space if a candidate from the candidate list is
        committed using a key for the label in front of the candidate
        (I.e. the using the 1-9 or F1-F9) or by clicking on a
        candidate with the mouse.

keys.name: inlinecompletion
keys.description:
Whether the best completion is first shown inline in the
        preëdit instead of showing a full candidate list. The inline
        candidate can be selected by typing Tab and then committed as
        usual, for example by typing Space or Control+Space. Typing
        Tab again moves to the next candidate and opens the full
        candidate list.

keys.name: autocapitalize
keys.description: Automatically capitalize after punctuation.

keys.name: pagesize
keys.description:
How many suggestion candidates to show in one page of the
        candidate list.

keys.name: lookuptableorientation
keys.description:
Whether the candidate window should be drawn horizontally or
        vertically.

keys.name: preeditunderline
keys.description: Which style of underlining to use for the preedit.

keys.name: preeditstyleonlywhenlookup
keys.summary: Use preedit style only if lookup is enabled
keys.description:
If this option is on, a preedit style like underlining will
        only be used if lookup is enabled.  The lookup can be disabled
        because one uses the option to enable lookup only when a key
        is pressed or because one uses the option to require a minimum
        number of characters before a lookup is done.

keys.name: mincharcomplete
keys.summary: Minimum number of chars for completion
keys.description:
Show no suggestions when less than this number of characters
        have been typed.

keys.name: useibuskeymap
keys.description:
Whether the use of an IBus keymap is forced while Typing
        Booster is active. For most users not using an IBus keymap is
        recommended. In that case, the keyboard layout which was
        active before switching to typing booster is used.

keys.name: ibuskeymap
keys.description:
The ibus keymap to use when forcing an IBus keymap while
        Typing Booster is active.

keys.name: debuglevel
keys.description:
When greater than 0, debug information may be printed to the
        log file and debug information may also be shown graphically.

keys.name: candidatesdelaymilliseconds
keys.description:
How long to wait after the last key press before
        candidates are displayed.

keys.name: ibuseventsleepseconds
keys.summary: Sleep time between some ibus events
keys.description:
Between some events sent to ibus like forward_key_event(),
        delete_surrounding_text(), commit_text(), a sleep is necessary.
        Without the sleep, these events may be processed out of order.

keys.name: shownumberofcandidates
keys.description:
Display how many candidates there are and which one is
        selected on top of the list of candidates.

keys.name: showstatusinfoinaux
keys.summary: Show status info in auxiliary text
keys.description:
Show in the auxiliary text whether “Emoji prediction” mode and
        “Off the record” mode are on or off and show which input
        method is currently used for the preëdit. The auxiliary text
        is an optional line of text displayed above the candidate
        list.

keys.name: autoselectcandidate
keys.description:
What you type will automatically be corrected to the best
        candidate by selecting the best candidate automatically. If
        this option is off you have to select a candidate manually by
        using the key binding to select the next candidate. If this
        option is on and you do not want to use the automatically
        selected candidate, you can use the key binding for the
        “cancel” command which will deselect all candidates.

keys.name: aichatenable
keys.description:
Enable or disable AI chat globally.
        When disabled, all AI chat related features and keybindings are turned off.

keys.name: aisystemmessage
keys.description: The message to start AI chats with.
keys.default_value: 'You are a large language model living in an input method and a helpful assistant. Respond concisely.'

keys.name: ollamamodel
keys.description: The model to use for ollama.

keys.name: ollamamaxcontext
keys.description:
The maximum number of ollama messages to keep as context when
        continuing a chat.

keys.name: inputmethodchangetimestamp

keys.name: dictionary

keys.description: Comma separated list of dictionaries to use.

keys.name: dictionaryinstalltimestamp
keys.summary: Time when a dictionary last was installed
keys.description: If that time stamp changes, reload all dictionaries.

keys.name: googleapplicationcredentials

keys.name: keybindings
keys.description: The list of configurable keybindings

keys.name: autosettings

keys.description: Set popup delay milliseconds to show IME switcher window. The default is 400. 0 = Show the window immediately. 0 < Delay milliseconds. 0 > Do not show the window and switch prev/next engines.

keys.name: version
keys.description: The saved version number will be used to check the difference between the version of the previous installed ibus and one of the current ibus.

keys.name: xkb-latin-layouts
keys.summary: Latin layouts which have no ASCII
keys.description: US layout is appended to the Latin layouts. variant can be omitted.

keys.name: use-xmodmap

keys.name: enable-by-default
keys.description: Enable input method by default when the application gets input focus

keys.name: dconf-preserve-name-prefixes
keys.description: Prefixes of DConf keys to stop name conversion

keys.name: trigger
keys.description: The shortcut keys for turning input method on or off

keys.name: triggers
keys.description: The shortcut keys for turning input method on or off

keys.name: enable-unconditional

	differently), “mm-hg” (millimeters of mercury), “inch-hg” (inches of mercury), “atm”
	(atmospheres).

	be shown. If empty, it will be taken from the locations database.
	The second field is the METAR code for the default weather station. It must not be
	empty and must correspond to a <code> tag in the Locations.xml file.
	The third field is a tuple of (latitude, longitude), to override the value taken
	from the database. This is only used for sunrise and moon phase calculations, not
	for weather forecast.

keys.name: mouse-tracking
keys.description:
Determines the position of the magnified mouse image within the
        magnified view and how it reacts to system mouse movement. The values
        are
        • none: no mouse tracking;
        • centered: the mouse image is
        displayed at the center of the zoom region (which also represents
        the point under the system mouse) and the magnified contents are
        scrolled as the system mouse moves;
        • proportional: the position of the magnified mouse in the zoom region
        is proportionally the same as the position of the system mouse on screen;
        • push: when the magnified mouse intersects a boundary of the zoom
        region, the contents are scrolled into view.

keys.name: focus-tracking
keys.description:
Determines the position of the focused widget within magnified view.

        The values are:

        • none: no focus tracking

        • centered: the focused image is displayed at the center of the zoom region (which also represents the
          point under the system focus) and the magnified contents are scrolled as the system focus moves

        • proportional: the position of the magnified focus in the zoom region is proportionally the same as the
          position of the system focus on screen

        • push: when the magnified focus intersects a boundary of the zoom region, the contents are scrolled
          into view

keys.name: caret-tracking
keys.description:
Determines the position of the caret within magnified view. The values are:

      • none: no caret tracking

      • centered: the image of the caret is displayed at the center of the zoom region (which also represents
        the point under the system caret) and the magnified contents are scrolled as the system caret moves

      • proportional: the position of the magnified caret in the zoom region is proportionally the same as the
        position of the system caret on screen

      • push: when the magnified caret intersects a boundary of the zoom region, the contents are scrolled into
        view

keys.name: screen-position
keys.description:
The magnified view either fills the entire screen, or occupies the
        top-half, bottom-half, left-half, or right-half of the screen.

keys.name: mag-factor
keys.description:
The power of the magnification. A value of 1.0 means no magnification.
        A value of 2.0 doubles the size.

keys.name: lens-mode
keys.description:
Whether the magnified view should be centered over the location of
        the system mouse and move with it.

keys.name: scroll-at-edges
keys.summary: Scroll magnified contents beyond the edges of the desktop
keys.description:
For centered mouse tracking, when the system pointer is at or near the
        edge of the screen, the magnified contents continue to scroll such that
        the screen edge moves into the magnified view.

keys.name: show-cross-hairs
keys.description:
Enables/disables display of crosshairs centered on the magnified

keys.name: cross-hairs-thickness
keys.summary: Thickness of the crosshairs in pixels

keys.name: cross-hairs-color
keys.description:

keys.name: cross-hairs-opacity

keys.name: cross-hairs-length
keys.summary: Length of the crosshairs in pixels
keys.description:

keys.name: cross-hairs-clip

keys.name: color-saturation

Represents a change to the color saturation, from 0.0 (grayscale)

keys.name: brightness-red

keys.name: brightness-green

keys.name: brightness-blue

keys.name: contrast-blue

keys.name: sources
keys.description:
List of input source identifiers available. Each source is
        specified as a tuple of 2 strings. The first string is the
        type and can be one of “xkb” or “ibus”. For “xkb” sources the
        second string is “xkb_layout+xkb_variant” or just “xkb_layout”
        if a XKB variant isn’t needed. For “ibus” sources the second
        string is the IBus engine name. An empty list means that the X
        server’s current XKB layout and variant won’t be touched and
        IBus won’t be used.

keys.name: xkb-options

keys.name: xkb-model

keys.name: toolbar-icons-size

keys.name: cursor-blink

keys.name: cursor-blink-timeout

keys.name: icon-theme

keys.name: gtk-key-theme
keys.description: Basename of the default keybinding theme used by gtk+.

keys.name: avatar-directories

keys.name: gtk-im-status-style
keys.description: Name of the GTK+ input method Status Style used by gtk+.

keys.description: Name of the input method module used by GTK+.

keys.name: document-font-name
keys.description: Name of the default font used for reading documents.

keys.name: monospace-font-name
keys.description:

keys.name: menubar-accel

keys.name: cursor-theme

keys.name: cursor-size
keys.description: Size of the cursor used as cursor theme.

keys.name: gtk-timeout-repeat
keys.description: Timeout in milliseconds between repeated clicks when a button is left pressed.

keys.name: gtk-color-palette

keys.description: Palette used in the color selector as defined by the “gtk-color-palette” setting

keys.name: gtk-color-scheme
keys.summary: List of symbolic names and color equivalents
keys.description: A “\n” separated list of “name:color” as defined by the “gtk-color-scheme” setting

keys.name: clock-format
keys.summary: Whether the clock displays in 24h or 12h format
keys.description: Whether the clock displays in 24h or 12h format

keys.name: clock-show-seconds
keys.description: If true, display seconds in the clock.

keys.name: clock-show-date
keys.description: If true, display date in the clock, in addition to time.

keys.name: clock-show-weekday
keys.description: If true, display weekday in the clock, in addition to time.

keys.name: enable-hot-corners
keys.description:
If true, the activities overview can be accessed by moving the

keys.name: show-battery-percentage
keys.description: If true, display battery percentage in the status menu, in addition to the icon.

keys.name: gtk-enable-primary-paste
keys.description: If true, gtk+ uses the primary paste selection, usually triggered by a middle mouse button click.

keys.name: overlay-scrolling
keys.description:
Whether scrollbars should be overlayed as indicators. Depending
        on input devices in use, permanent scrollbars may still be
        displayed.

keys.name: locate-pointer
keys.summary: Highlights the current location of the pointer.
keys.description: If true, pressing a key will highlight the current pointer location on screen.

keys.name: font-antialiasing
keys.description: The type of antialiasing to use when rendering fonts. Possible values are: “none” for no antialiasing, “grayscale” for standard grayscale antialiasing, and “rgba” for subpixel antialiasing (LCD screens only).

keys.name: font-hinting
keys.description: The type of hinting to use when rendering fonts. Possible values are: “none” for no hinting and “slight” for fitting only to the Y-axis like Microsoft’s ClearType, DirectWrite and Adobe’s proprietary font rendering engine. Ignores native hinting within the font, generates hints algorithmically. Used on Ubuntu by default. Recommended. The meaning of “medium” and “full” depends on the font format (.ttf, .otf, .pfa/.pfb) and the installed version of FreeType. They usually try to fit glyphs to both the X and the Y axis (except for .otf: Y-only). This can lead to distortion and/or inconsistent rendering depending on the quality of the font, the font format and the state of FreeType’s font engines.

keys.name: font-rgba-order
keys.description: The order of subpixel elements on an LCD screen; only used when antialiasing is set to “rgba”. Possible values are: “rgb” for red on left (most common), “bgr” for blue on left, “vrgb” for red on top, “vbgr” for red on bottom.

keys.name: color-scheme
keys.description: The preferred color scheme for the user interface. Valid values are “default”, “prefer-dark”, “prefer-light”.

keys.name: font-rendering
keys.description:
Preference to indicate whether font rendering should follow the low-level `font-hinting` and `font-antialiasing` and `font-rgba-order` settings, or take environmental factors such as screen resolution and scaling into account.
        Possible values are: "manual" for respecting the low-level settings, or "automatic" for letting the toolkit
        make its own decisions.

keys.name: accent-color
keys.description: The preferred accent color for the user interface. Valid values are "blue", "teal", "green", "yellow", "orange", "red", "pink", "purple", "slate".

keys.name: disable-command-line
