        line to be executed. For example, this would disable access to the 
        panel’s “Run Application” dialog.

keys.name: disable-save-to-disk
keys.description:
Prevent the user from saving files to disk. For example, this would 
        disable access to all applications’ “Save as” dialogs.

keys.name: disable-printing
keys.description:
Prevent the user from printing. For example, this would disable access 
        to all applications’ “Print” dialogs.

keys.name: disable-print-setup
keys.description:
Prevent the user from modifying print settings. For example, this would 
        disable access to all applications’ “Print Setup” dialogs.

keys.name: disable-user-switching
keys.description:
Prevent the user from switching to another account while their session 
        is active.

keys.name: disable-lock-screen
keys.description: Prevent the user from locking their screen.

keys.name: disable-application-handlers
keys.summary: Disable URL and MIME type handlers
keys.description: Prevent running any URL or MIME type handler applications.

keys.name: disable-log-out
keys.description: Prevent the user from logging out.

keys.name: user-administration-disabled
keys.description:
Stop the user from modifying user accounts. By default, we allow adding
        and removing users, as well as changing other users settings.

keys.name: mount-removable-storage-devices-as-read-only
keys.summary: Mount removable storage devices as read-only
keys.description:
Prevent users from writing or modifying files on removable storage
        devices (i.e. flash disks, mobile phones, cameras).

keys.name: disable-show-password
keys.description: Disable the "Show Password" menu item in password entries.

keys.name: automount
keys.description: If set to true, then Nautilus will automatically mount media such as user-visible hard disks and removable media on start-up and media insertion.

keys.name: automount-open
keys.summary: Whether to automatically open a folder for automounted media
keys.description: If set to true, then Nautilus will automatically open a folder when media is automounted. This only applies to media where no known x-content/* type was detected; for media where a known x-content type is detected, the user configurable action will be taken instead.

keys.name: autorun-never
keys.summary: Never prompt or autorun/autostart programs when media are inserted
keys.description: If set to true, then Nautilus will never prompt nor autorun/autostart programs when a medium is inserted.

keys.name: autorun-x-content-start-app
keys.summary: List of x-content/* types where the preferred application will be launched
keys.description: List of x-content/* types for which the user have chosen to start an application in the preference capplet. The preferred application for the given type will be started on insertion on media matching these types.

keys.name: autorun-x-content-ignore
keys.summary: List of x-content/* types set to “Do Nothing”
keys.description: List of x-content/* types for which the user have chosen “Do Nothing” in the preference capplet. No prompt will be shown nor will any matching application be started on insertion of media matching these types.

keys.name: autorun-x-content-open-folder
keys.summary: List of x-content/* types set to “Open Folder”
keys.description: List of x-content/* types for which the user have chosen “Open Folder” in the preferences capplet. A folder window will be opened on insertion of media matching these types.

keys.name: show-banners
keys.description: Whether notification banners are visible for application notifications.

keys.name: show-in-lock-screen
keys.summary: Show notifications in the lock screen
keys.description: Whether notifications are shown in the lock screen or not.

keys.name: application-id
keys.description: The application that this policy is for.

keys.name: enable
keys.description: Whether notifications are globally enabled for this application.

keys.name: enable-sound-alerts
keys.description: Whether notifications should be accompanied by sound alerts.

keys.name: show-banners
keys.description:
Whether notification banners for this application are shown or not. Does not
        affect clicking on message tray buttons.

keys.name: force-expanded
keys.description: Whether notifications from this application are expanded automatically when in banner mode.

keys.name: show-in-lock-screen
keys.description: Whether notifications from this application are shown when the screen is locked.

keys.name: details-in-lock-screen
keys.summary: Show details in the lock screen
keys.description:
Whether the summary and body of notifications from this application will be
        visible in the locked screen.

keys.name: edge-scrolling-enabled
keys.description: When enabled, touchpads that support edge scrolling will have that feature enabled.

keys.name: two-finger-scrolling-enabled
keys.description: When enabled, touchpads that support two-finger scrolling will have that feature enabled.

keys.name: disable-while-typing
keys.summary: Whether to disable the touchpad while typing
keys.description: When enabled, touchpads will be disabled while typing on the keyboard.

keys.name: tap-to-click
keys.description: Set this to TRUE to be able to send mouse clicks by tapping on the touchpad.

keys.name: tap-button-map
keys.description: Defines the mapping between the number of fingers and touchpad buttons. The default is to have a 1, 2 and 3 finger tap to map to the left, right and middle button ("lrm"), respectively.

keys.name: tap-and-drag
keys.description: Set this to TRUE to be able to start a drag by tapping and immediately moving the finger that’s now pressed on the touchpad.

keys.name: tap-and-drag-lock
keys.description: Set this to TRUE to lock the dragging process within a short timeout when the finger is lifted on the touchpad and the tap-and-drag setting is enabled.

keys.name: send-events
keys.description: Defines the situations in which the touchpad is enabled.

keys.name: left-handed
keys.description: Swap left and right mouse buttons for left-handed mice with “left”, “right” for right-handed, “mouse” to follow the mouse setting.

keys.name: accel-profile
keys.description: Acceleration profile used for connected touchpads. The acceleration profile can be set to either default (“default”) which uses the default acceleration profile for each device, flat (“flat”), which accelerates by a device specific constant factor derived from the configured pointer speed, or adaptive (“adaptive”) which adapts the acceleration depending on the mouse movement. If a touchpad doesn’t support the configured profile, “default” will be used.

keys.name: speed
keys.description: Pointer speed for the touchpad. Accepted values are in the [-1..1] range (from “unaccelerated” to “fast”). A value of 0 is the system default.

keys.name: natural-scroll
keys.description: Set this to TRUE to enable natural (reverse) scrolling for touchpads.

keys.name: click-method
keys.description: How to generate software-emulated buttons, either disabled (“none”), through specific areas (“areas”), number of fingers (“fingers”) or left as hardware default (“default”).

keys.name: middle-click-emulation
keys.description: Set this to TRUE to enable middle click during simultaneous left and right click.

keys.name: repeat
keys.description: Set this to TRUE to enable keyboard repeat.

keys.name: repeat-interval
keys.description: Delay between repeats in milliseconds.

keys.name: delay
keys.description: Initial key repeat delay in milliseconds.

keys.name: remember-numlock-state
keys.description: When set to true, GNOME will remember the state of the NumLock LED between sessions.

keys.name: numlock-state
keys.description: The remembered state of the NumLock LED.

keys.name: left-handed
keys.description: Swap left and right mouse buttons for left-handed mice.

keys.name: speed
keys.description: Pointer speed for mice. Accepted values are in the [-1..1] range (from “unaccelerated” to “fast”). A value of 0 is the system default.

keys.name: natural-scroll
keys.description: Set this to TRUE to enable natural (reverse) scrolling for mice.

keys.name: accel-profile
keys.description: Acceleration profile used for connected mice. The acceleration profile can be set to either default (“default”) which uses the default acceleration profile for each device, flat (“flat”), which accelerates by a device specific constant factor derived from the configured pointer speed, or adaptive (“adaptive”) which adapts the acceleration depending on the mouse movement. If a mouse doesn’t support the configured profile, “default” will be used.

keys.name: middle-click-emulation
keys.description: Set this to TRUE to enable middle click during simultaneous left and right click.

keys.name: double-click
keys.description: Length of a double click in milliseconds.

keys.name: drag-threshold
keys.description: Distance before a drag is started.

keys.name: output
keys.description: EDID information of the output the tablet is mapped to. Must be in the format [vendor, product, serial]. ["","",""] relies on automatic mapping.

keys.name: mapping
keys.description: How input affects the pointer on the screen

keys.name: area
keys.description: Dead area padding around the active area, in percentages. Respectively applied to left,right,top and bottom sides.

keys.name: keep-aspect
keys.description: Enable this to restrict the tablet area to match the aspect ratio of the output.

keys.name: left-handed
keys.description: Enable this to allow physically rotating the tablet for left-handed setups

keys.name: pressure-curve
keys.description: Set this to x1, y1 and x2, y2 of the pressure curve applied to the stylus.

keys.name: pressure-range
keys.description: Set this to the percentage of the physical pressure range that should map to the logical range.

keys.name: eraser-pressure-curve
keys.description: Set this to x1, y1 and x2, y2 of the pressure curve applied to the eraser.

keys.name: eraser-pressure-range
keys.description: Set this to the percentage of the physical pressure range that should map to the logical range.

keys.name: button-action
keys.description: Stylus button action, this button is located along the pen handle.

keys.name: secondary-button-action
keys.description: Secondary stylus button action, this button is located along the pen handle on some styli like the Grip Pen. Other styli like the Airbrush Pen or the Inking Pen only have one button, this setting is ineffective on those.

keys.name: tertiary-button-action
keys.description: Tertiary stylus button action, this button is located along the pen handle on some styli like the 3D Pen. Other styli like the Grip Pen only have two buttons, this setting is ineffective on those.

keys.name: button-keybinding
keys.summary: Key combination for the primary button
keys.description: The keyboard shortcut generated when the button is pressed for custom actions.

keys.name: secondary-button-keybinding
keys.summary: Key combination for the secondary button
keys.description: The keyboard shortcut generated when the button is pressed for custom actions.

keys.name: tertiary-button-keybinding
keys.summary: Key combination for the tertiary button
keys.description: The keyboard shortcut generated when the button is pressed for custom actions.

keys.name: action
keys.description: The type of action triggered by the button being pressed.

keys.name: keybinding
keys.summary: Key combination for the custom action
keys.description: The keyboard shortcut generated when the button is pressed for custom actions.

keys.name: output
keys.description: EDID information of the output the touchscreen is mapped to. Must be in the format [vendor, product, serial]. ["","",""] relies on automatic mapping.

keys.name: scroll-wheel-emulation-button
keys.summary: Mouse wheel emulation button. 0 to disable the feature.

keys.name: scroll-wheel-emulation-button-lock
keys.description: Set this to TRUE to enable button locking for the wheel emulation button. If enabled, the first click of the button enables scrolling, the second click disables scrolling again.

keys.name: accel-profile
keys.description: Acceleration profile used for the trackball. The acceleration profile can be set to either default (“default”) which uses the default acceleration profile, flat (“flat”), which accelerates by a device specific constant factor derived from the configured speed, or adaptive (“adaptive”) which adapts the acceleration depending on the movement. If the trackball doesn’t support the configured profile, “default” will be used.

keys.name: middle-click-emulation
keys.description: Set this to TRUE to enable middle click during simultaneous left and right click.

keys.name: speed
keys.description: Pointer speed for pointing sticks. Accepted values are in the [-1..1] range (from “unaccelerated” to “fast”). A value of 0 is the system default.

keys.name: accel-profile
keys.description: Acceleration profile used for the pointing stick. The acceleration profile can be set to either default (“default”) which uses the default acceleration profile, flat (“flat”), which accelerates by a device specific constant factor derived from the configured speed, or adaptive (“adaptive”) which adapts the acceleration depending on the movement. If the pointing stick doesn’t support the configured profile, “default” will be used.

keys.name: scroll-method
keys.description: Scrolling method used for pointing sticks. The scrolling method can be set to either default (“default”) which uses the default method, none (“none”), which disables scrolling, or middle-button (“on-button-down”) which enables scrolling whilst the middle button is held down.

keys.name: hide-identity
keys.description:
If set to true, the system will make an effort to
      not divulge the user’s identity on screen or on the network.

keys.name: show-full-name-in-top-bar
keys.summary: Show full name in the user menu
keys.description: Whether the users full name is shown in the user menu or not.

keys.name: remove-old-trash-files
keys.summary: Whether to remove old files from the trash automatically
keys.description:
If TRUE, automatically remove files from the trash
      when they are older than “old-files-age” days.

keys.name: remove-old-temp-files
keys.summary: Whether to remove old temporary files automatically
keys.description:
If TRUE, automatically remove temporary files
      when they are older than “old-files-age” days.

keys.name: old-files-age
keys.summary: Number of days to keep trash and temporary files
keys.description:
Consider trash and temporary files old after
      this many days.

keys.name: remember-recent-files
keys.summary: Whether to remember recently used files
keys.description:
If FALSE, applications will not remember recently
        used files.

keys.name: recent-files-max-age
keys.summary: Number of days to remember recently used files for
keys.description:
Recently used files will be remembered for this many days.
        If set to 0, recent files will not be remembered; if set to -1, they
        will be retained indefinitely.

keys.name: remember-app-usage
keys.description:
If FALSE, application usage will not be monitored
        and recorded.

keys.name: send-software-usage-stats
keys.summary: Send statistics when applications are removed or installed
keys.description: If FALSE, no anonymous installation or removal information will be sent to the vendor.

keys.name: report-technical-problems
keys.summary: Send reports of technical problems to the vendor
keys.description: If TRUE, anonymized reports will be sent automatically to the vendor.

keys.name: disable-microphone
keys.summary: Don’t allow applications to access the microphone
keys.description: If TRUE, applications should not use the microphone.

keys.name: disable-camera
keys.summary: Don’t allow applications to access the camera
keys.description: If TRUE, applications should not use the camera.

keys.name: disable-sound-output
keys.summary: Don’t allow applications to output sound
keys.description: If TRUE, applications should not make sound.

keys.name: usb-protection
keys.description:
If the USBGuard service is present and this setting is
        enabled, USB devices will be protected as configured in the
        usb-protection-level setting.

keys.name: usb-protection-level
keys.summary: When USB devices should be rejected
keys.description:
If set to “lockscreen”, only when the lock screen is present new USB devices will
        be rejected; if set to “always”, all new USB devices will always be rejected.

keys.name: privacy-screen
keys.summary: Whether the privacy screen is enabled
keys.description:
If the underlying hardware has privacy screen support and
        this setting is enabled, the panels supporting this technology will be
        obscured from lateral view.

keys.name: port
keys.summary: The port used by the RDP server
keys.description: The RDP client will connect to this port to use this RDP server.

keys.name: negotiate-port
keys.summary: Search a different RDP port if the configured one is used
keys.description:
When negotiate-port is set to 'true' the RDP server will attempt to
        listen to the first available of the next 10 ports starting from the
        configured one.

keys.name: enable
keys.summary: Whether the RDP backend is enabled or not
keys.description: If set to 'true' the RDP backend will be initialized.

keys.name: screen-share-mode
keys.description:
The screenshare mode specifies, whether the RDP backend mirrors the
        primary screen, or whether a virtual monitor is created.

        For the initial resolution of the virtual monitor, the RDP backend uses
        either the client core data ([MS-RDPBCGR] 2.2.1.3.2) or the client
        monitor data ([MS-RDPBCGR] 2.2.1.3.6), depending on what is available.

        When using a remote desktop session with a virtual monitor, clients can
        resize the resolution of the virtual monitor during a session with the
        Display Update Virtual Channel Extension ([MS-RDPEDISP]).

        Allowed screenshare modes include:

         * mirror-primary - Record the primary monitor of the current user
                            session.

         * extend - Create a new virtual monitor and use it for the remote
                    desktop session.
                    The resolution of this virtual monitor is derived from the
                    monitor configuration, submitted by the remote desktop
                    client.

keys.name: tls-cert
keys.description:
In order to be able to use RDP with TLS Security, both the private key
        file and the certificate file need to be provided to the RDP server.

keys.name: tls-key
keys.summary: Path to the private key file
keys.description:
In order to be able to use RDP with TLS Security, both the private key
        file and the certificate file need to be provided to the RDP server.

keys.name: view-only
keys.summary: Only allow remote connections to view the screen content
keys.description:
When view-only is true, remote RDP connections cannot manipulate input
        devices (e.g. mouse and keyboard).

keys.name: port
keys.summary: The port used by the RDP server
keys.description: The RDP client will connect to this port to use this RDP server.

keys.name: negotiate-port
keys.summary: Search a different RDP port if the configured one is used
keys.description:
When negotiate-port is set to 'true' the RDP server will attempt to
        listen to the first available of the next 10 ports starting from the
        configured one.

keys.name: enable
keys.summary: Whether the RDP backend is enabled or not
keys.description: If set to 'true' the RDP backend will be initialized.

keys.name: port
keys.summary: The port used by the VNC server
keys.description: The VNC client will connect to this port to use this VNC server.

keys.name: negotiate-port
keys.summary: Search a different VNC port if the configured one is used
keys.description:
When negotiate-port is set to 'true' the VNC server will attempt to
        listen to the first available of the next 10 ports starting from the
        configured one.

keys.name: enable
keys.summary: Whether the VNC backend is enabled or not
keys.description: If set to 'true' the VNC backend will be initialized.

keys.name: view-only
keys.summary: Only allow remote connections to view the screen content
keys.description:
When view-only is true, remote VNC connections cannot manipulate input
        devices (e.g. mouse and keyboard).

keys.name: auth-method
keys.summary: Method used to authenticate VNC connections
keys.description:
The VNC authentication method describes how a remote connection is
        authenticated. It can currently be done in two different ways:

         * prompt   - by prompting the user for each new connection, requiring a
                      person with physical access to the workstation to
                      explicitly approve the new connection.
         * password - by requiring the remote client to provide a known password

keys.name: screen-share-mode
keys.description:
The screenshare mode specifies, whether the VNC backend mirrors the
        primary screen, or whether a virtual monitor is created.

        For the initial resolution of the virtual monitor, the VNC backend
        uses a default size of 1920x1080 pixels.

        When using a remote desktop session with a virtual monitor, clients can
        resize the resolution of the virtual monitor during a session with the
        setDesktopSize PDU.

        Allowed screenshare modes include:

         * mirror-primary - Record the primary monitor of the current user
                            session.

         * extend - Create a new virtual monitor and use it for the remote
                    desktop session. The initial monitor resolution of this
                    virtual monitor is set to 1920x1080 pixels. Clients can
                    override the initial resolution with subsequent monitor
                    configuration updates.

keys.name: encryption
keys.description:
Allowed encryption methods. Includes the following:

	 * none     - no encryption
	 * tls-anon - anonymous (unauthenticated) TLS

keys.name: port
keys.summary: The port used by the VNC server
keys.description: The VNC client will connect to this port to use this VNC server.

keys.name: negotiate-port
keys.summary: Search a different VNC port if the configured one is used
keys.description:
When negotiate-port is set to 'true' the VNC server will attempt to
        listen to the first available of the next 10 ports starting from the
        configured one.

keys.name: enable
keys.summary: Whether the VNC backend is enabled or not
keys.description: If set to 'true' the VNC backend will be initialized.

keys.name: history-enabled
keys.description: Enable basic screen time functionality, recording usage data for viewing history.

keys.name: daily-limit-enabled
keys.description: Enable imposing a screen time limit on the user, as set in daily-limit-seconds.

keys.name: daily-limit-seconds
keys.description: The limit on the amount of daily screen time the user is allowed, in seconds.

keys.name: grayscale
keys.description: Whether to make the screen grayscale once the user’s screen time limit is reached.

keys.name: restart-enabled
keys.description: Allow shutdown and restart actions from the lock screen.

keys.name: idle-activation-enabled
keys.description:
Set this to TRUE to activate the screensaver when the session is idle.

        DEPRECATED: This key is deprecated and ignored.
        Set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay to 0 if you do not want to activate the screensaver.

keys.name: lock-enabled
keys.description: Set this to TRUE to lock the screen when the screensaver goes active.

keys.name: lock-delay
keys.description: The number of seconds after screensaver activation before locking the screen.

keys.name: show-full-name-in-top-bar
keys.summary: Show full name in the lock screen
keys.description: Whether the user’s full name is shown in the lock screen or not. This only affects the screen shield, the name is always shown in the unlock dialog.

keys.name: embedded-keyboard-enabled
keys.summary: Allow embedding a keyboard into the window
keys.description:
Set this to TRUE to allow embedding a keyboard into the window when trying to unlock.  The “keyboard_command” key must be set with the appropriate command.

        DEPRECATED: This key is deprecated and ignored.

keys.name: embedded-keyboard-command
keys.description:
The command that will be run, if the “embedded_keyboard_enabled” key is set to TRUE, to embed a keyboard widget into the window. This command should implement an XEMBED plug interface and output a window XID on the standard output.

        DEPRECATED: This key is deprecated and ignored.

keys.name: logout-enabled
keys.description:
Set this to TRUE to offer an option in the unlock dialog to allow logging out after a delay.  The delay is specified in the “logout_delay” key.

        DEPRECATED: This key is deprecated and ignored.

keys.name: logout-delay
keys.description:
The number of seconds after the screensaver activation before a logout option will appear in the unlock dialog. This key has effect only if the “logout_enable” key is set to TRUE.

        DEPRECATED: This key is deprecated and ignored

keys.name: logout-command
keys.description:
The command to invoke when the logout button is clicked.  This command should simply log the user out without any interaction. This key has effect only if the “logout_enable” key is set to TRUE.

        DEPRECATED: This key is deprecated and ignored.

keys.name: user-switch-enabled
keys.description: Set this to TRUE to offer an option in the unlock dialog to switch to a different user account.

keys.name: status-message-enabled
keys.summary: Allow the session status message to be displayed
keys.description:
Allow the session status message to be displayed when the screen is locked.

        DEPRECATED: This key is deprecated and ignored.

keys.name: picture-options
keys.description:
Determines how the image set by wallpaper_filename is rendered.
        Possible values are “none”, “wallpaper”, “centered”, “scaled”,
        “stretched”, “zoom”, “spanned”.

keys.name: picture-uri
keys.description:
URI to use for the background image. Note that the backend only supports
        local (file://) URIs.

keys.name: picture-opacity
keys.description: Opacity with which to draw the background picture.

keys.name: primary-color
keys.description: Left or Top color when drawing gradients, or the solid color.

keys.name: secondary-color
keys.description: Right or Bottom color when drawing gradients, not used for solid color.

keys.name: color-shading-type
keys.description:
How to shade the background color. Possible values are “horizontal”,
        “vertical”, and “solid”.

keys.name: disable-external
keys.description:
Set to true to disable all external search provider programs,
        whether or not they are independently disabled or enabled.
        External search providers are installed by applications in
        $XDG_DATA_DIR/gnome-shell/search-providers.

keys.name: disabled
keys.summary:
List of desktop file IDs for which the associated default-enabled search provider
        should be disabled
keys.description:
Results for applications contained in this list will not be displayed
        when searching.

keys.name: enabled
keys.summary:
List of desktop file IDs for which the associated default-disabled search provider
        should be enabled
keys.description:
Results for applications contained in this list will be displayed
        when searching.

keys.name: sort-order
keys.summary: List of desktop file IDs for search providers sort order
keys.description:
Results for applications contained in this list will be displayed in
        the specified order.
        Results for applications not specified in this list will be displayed
        last, sorted alphabetically.

keys.name: idle-delay
keys.summary: Time before session is considered idle
keys.description: The number of seconds of inactivity before the session is considered idle.

keys.name: session-name
keys.description:
The name of the session to use.  Known value is
      “gnome”.

keys.name: event-sounds
keys.description: Whether to play sounds on user events.

keys.name: theme-name
keys.description: The XDG sound theme to use for event sounds.

keys.name: input-feedback-sounds
keys.description: Whether to play sounds on input events.

keys.name: allow-volume-above-100-percent
keys.description: Whether volume can be set above 100%, using software amplification.

keys.name: maximum-age
keys.description: Maximum age for thumbnails in the cache, in days. Set to -1 to disable cleaning.

keys.name: maximum-size
keys.description: Maximum size of the thumbnail cache, in megabytes. Set to -1 to disable cleaning.

keys.name: disable-all
keys.description:
Set to true to disable all external thumbnailer programs,
        independent on whether they are independently disabled/enabled.

keys.name: disable
keys.summary:
List of mime-types for which external thumbnailer
        programs will be disabled
keys.description:
Thumbnails will not be created for files whose mime-type is
        contained in the list.

keys.name: switch-group-backward
keys.summary: Reverse switch windows of an application

keys.name: cycle-group
keys.summary: Switch windows of an app directly

keys.name: cycle-group-backward
keys.summary: Reverse switch windows of an app directly

keys.name: panel-main-menu
keys.summary: DEPRECATED: This key is deprecated and ignored.

keys.name: toggle-above
keys.summary: Toggle window always appearing on top

keys.name: toggle-on-all-workspaces
keys.summary: Toggle window on all workspaces or one

keys.name: move-to-workspace-left
keys.summary: Move window one workspace to the left

keys.name: move-to-workspace-right
keys.summary: Move window one workspace to the right

keys.name: move-to-monitor-left
keys.summary: Move window to the next monitor on the left

keys.name: move-to-monitor-right
keys.summary: Move window to the next monitor on the right

keys.name: move-to-monitor-up
keys.summary: Move window to the next monitor above

keys.name: move-to-monitor-down
keys.summary: Move window to the next monitor below

keys.name: raise-or-lower
keys.summary: Raise window if covered, otherwise lower it

keys.name: move-to-corner-nw
keys.summary: Move window to top left corner

keys.name: move-to-corner-ne
keys.summary: Move window to top right corner

keys.name: move-to-corner-sw
keys.summary: Move window to bottom left corner

keys.name: move-to-corner-se
keys.summary: Move window to bottom right corner

keys.name: move-to-side-n
keys.summary: Move window to top edge of screen

keys.name: move-to-side-s
keys.summary: Move window to bottom edge of screen

keys.name: move-to-side-e
keys.summary: Move window to right side of screen

keys.name: move-to-side-w
keys.summary: Move window to left side of screen

keys.name: move-to-center
keys.summary: Move window to center of screen

keys.name: switch-input-source
keys.description: Binding to select the next input source

keys.name: switch-input-source-backward
keys.description: Binding to select the previous input source

keys.name: always-on-top
keys.summary: Toggle window to be always on top
keys.description: Set or unset window to appear always on top

keys.name: mouse-button-modifier
keys.summary: Modifier to use for modified window click actions
keys.description:
Clicking a window while holding down this modifier key  will move the
        window (left click), resize the window  (middle click), or show the
        window menu (right click). The middle and right click operations may
        be swapped using the “resize-with-right-button” key. Modifier is
        expressed as “<Alt>” or “<Super>” for example.

keys.name: resize-with-right-button
keys.summary: Whether to resize with the right button
keys.description:
Set this to true to resize with the right button and show a menu with
        the middle button while holding down the key given in
        “mouse-button-modifier”; set it to false to make it work
        the opposite way around.

keys.name: button-layout
keys.summary: Arrangement of buttons on the titlebar
keys.description:
Arrangement of buttons on the titlebar. The value should be a string,
        such as  “menu:minimize,maximize,spacer,close”; the colon separates
        the  left corner of the window from the right corner, and  the button
        names are comma-separated. Duplicate buttons are not allowed. Unknown
        button names are silently ignored so that buttons can be added in
        future metacity versions  without breaking older versions. A special
        spacer tag can be used to insert some space between
        two adjacent buttons.

keys.name: focus-mode
keys.description:
The window focus mode indicates how windows are activated.  It has
        three possible values; “click” means windows must  be clicked in order
        to focus them, “sloppy” means windows are focused when the mouse enters
        the window, and “mouse” means windows are focused when the mouse enters
        the window and  unfocused when the mouse leaves the window.

keys.name: focus-new-windows
keys.summary: Control how new windows get focus
keys.description:
This option provides additional control over how newly created windows
        get focus.  It has two possible values; “smart” applies the user’s
        normal focus mode, and “strict” results in new windows not being given
        focus automatically.

keys.name: raise-on-click
keys.summary: Whether windows should be raised when their client area is clicked
keys.description:
The default, true, indicates that a window will be raised
	whenever its client area or its frame is clicked.

	Setting this to false means that a window will not be raised
	if it is clicked on the client area.  To raise it, one can
	click anywhere in the window’s frame, or Super-click on any
	part of the window.  This mode is useful if one uses many overlapping
	windows.

keys.name: action-double-click-titlebar
keys.description:
This option determines the effects of double-clicking on the title bar.

        Current valid options are “toggle-maximize” which will
        maximize/unmaximize the window, “toggle-maximize-horizontally” and
        “toggle-maximize-vertically” which will maximize/unmaximize the window
        in that direction only, “minimize” which will minimize the window,
        “menu” which will display the window menu, “lower” which will put the
        window behind all the others, and “none” which will not do anything.

keys.name: action-middle-click-titlebar
keys.description:
This option determines the effects of middle-clicking on the title bar.

        Current valid options are “toggle-maximize” which will
        maximize/unmaximize the window,
        “toggle-maximize-horizontally” and “toggle-maximize-vertically”
        which will maximize/unmaximize the window in that direction only,
        “minimize” which will minimize the window, “menu” which will display
        the window menu, “lower” which will put the window behind all the
        others, and “none” which will not do anything.

keys.name: action-right-click-titlebar
keys.description:
This option determines the effects of right-clicking on the title bar.

        Current valid options are “toggle-maximize” which will
        maximize/unmaximize the window, “toggle-maximize-horizontally” and
        “toggle-maximize-vertically” which will maximize/unmaximize
        the window in that direction only, “minimize” which will minimize
        the window, “menu” which will display the window menu, “lower” which
        will put the window behind all the others, and “none” which will not do
        anything.

keys.name: auto-raise
keys.description:
If set to true, and the focus mode is either “sloppy” or “mouse”
        then the focused window will be automatically raised after a delay
        specified by the auto-raise-delay key. This is not related to clicking
        on a window to raise it, nor to entering a window during drag-and-drop.

keys.name: auto-raise-delay
keys.summary: Delay in milliseconds for the auto raise option
keys.description:
The time delay before raising a window if auto-raise is set to true.
        The delay is given in thousandths of a second.

keys.name: theme
keys.description:
The theme determines the appearance of window borders, titlebar,
        and so forth.

        DEPRECATED: This key is deprecated and ignored.

keys.name: titlebar-uses-system-font
keys.summary: Use standard system font in window titles
keys.description:
If true, ignore the titlebar-font option, and use the standard
        application font for window titles.

keys.name: titlebar-font
keys.description:
A font description string describing a font for window titlebars.
        The size from the description will only be used if the
        titlebar-font-size option is set to 0. Also, this option is disabled
        if the titlebar-uses-desktop-font option is set to true.

keys.name: num-workspaces
keys.description:
Number of workspaces. Must be more than zero, and has a fixed maximum
        to prevent making the desktop unusable by accidentally asking
        for too many workspaces.

keys.name: audible-bell
keys.description:
Determines whether applications or the system can generate audible
        “beeps”; may be used in conjunction with “visual bell”
        to allow silent “beeps”.

keys.name: visual-bell
keys.description:
Turns on a visual indication when an application or the system issues
        a “bell” or “beep”; useful for the hard-of-hearing and for use
        in noisy environments.

keys.name: visual-bell-type
keys.description:
Tells the WM how to implement the visual indication that the
        system bell or another application “bell” indicator has been rung.

        Currently there are two valid values, “fullscreen-flash”, which
        causes a fullscreen white-black flash, and “frame-flash” which
        causes the titlebar of the application which sent the bell signal
        to flash.

        If the application which sent the bell is unknown (as is usually
        the case for the default “system beep”), the currently focused
        window’s titlebar is flashed.

keys.name: disable-workarounds
keys.summary: Disable misfeatures that are required by old or broken applications
keys.description:
Some applications disregard specifications in ways that result in
        window manager misfeatures. This option puts the WM in a
        rigorously correct mode, which gives a more consistent
        user interface, provided one does not need to run any
        misbehaving applications.

keys.name: workspace-names
keys.description:
Defines the names that should be assigned to workspaces.
        If the list is too long for the current number of workspaces,
        names in excess will be ignored. If the list is too short, or
        includes empty names, missing values will be replaced with the
        default (“Workspace N”).

keys.name: homepage-url
keys.description: Address of the user’s home page.

keys.name: default-search-engine
keys.description: Name of the search engine selected by default.

keys.name: incognito-search-engine
keys.description: Name of the search engine selected in incognito mode.

keys.name: search-engine-providers
keys.description:
List of the search engines. It is an array of vardicts with each
			  vardict corresponding to a search engine, and with the following
			  supported keys: "name" is the name of the search engine. "url" is
			  the search URL with the search term replaced with %s. "bang" is the
			  bang (shortcut word) of the search engine.

keys.name: use-search-suggestions
keys.description: Whether to show Search Suggestion in url entry popdown.

keys.name: new-windows-in-tabs
keys.summary: Force new windows to be opened in tabs
keys.description: Force new window requests to be opened in tabs instead of using a new window.

keys.name: restore-session-policy
keys.summary: Whether to automatically restore the last session
keys.description: Defines how the session will be restored during startup. Allowed values are “always” (the previous state of the application is always restored) and “crashed” (the session is only restored if the application crashes).

keys.name: restore-session-delaying-loads
keys.summary: Whether to delay loading of tabs that are not immediately visible on session restore
keys.description: When this option is set to true, tabs will not start loading until the user switches to them, upon session restore.

keys.name: content-filters
keys.description: List of URLs with content filtering rules in JSON format to be used by the ad blocker.

keys.name: ask-for-default
keys.summary: Whether to ask for setting browser as default
keys.description: When this option is set to true, browser will ask for being default if it is not already set.

keys.name: start-in-incognito-mode
keys.description: When this option is set to true, browser will always start in incognito mode

keys.name: active-clear-data-items
keys.description: Selection (bitmask) which clear data items should be active by default. 1 = Cookies, 2 = HTTP disk cache, 4 = Local storage data, 8 = Offline web application cache, 16 = IndexDB databases, 32 = WebSQL databases, 64 = Plugins data, 128 = HSTS policies cache, 256 = Intelligent Tracking Prevention data.

keys.name: expand-tabs-bar
keys.summary: Expand tabs size to fill the available space on the tabs bar.
keys.description: If enabled the tabs will expand to use the entire available space in the tabs bar. This setting is ignored in Pantheon desktop.

keys.name: bottom-url-bar
keys.description: Whether to show url bar on bottom.

keys.name: tabs-bar-visibility-policy
keys.summary: The visibility policy for the tabs  bar.
keys.description: Controls when the tabs bar is shown. Possible values are “always” (the tabs bar is always shown), “more-than-one” (the tabs bar is only shown if there’s two or more tabs) and “never” (the tabs bar is never shown). This setting is ignored in Pantheon desktop, and “always” value is used.

keys.name: keep-window-open
keys.summary: Keep window open when closing last tab
keys.description: If enabled application window is kept open when closing the last tab.

keys.name: webkit-features-page
keys.summary: Show the WebKit features preferences page.
keys.description: Controls whether the preferences window shows the page which allows toggling experimental and development WebKit features.

keys.name: webkit-features-page-show-internal
keys.summary: Show internal WebKit features in the features preferences page.
keys.description: Controls whether the features page in the preferences window shows internal WebKit features.

keys.name: font-style
keys.description: Chooses the style of the main body text for articles in reader mode. Possible values are “sans” and “serif”.

keys.name: color-scheme
keys.description: Selects the style of colors for articles displayed in reader mode. Possible values are “light” (dark text on light background) and “dark” (light text on dark background). This setting is ignored on systems that provide a system-wide dark style preference, such as GNOME 42 and newer.

keys.name: use-gnome-fonts
keys.description: Use GNOME font settings.

keys.name: sans-serif-font
keys.description: A value to be used to override sans-serif desktop font when use-gnome-fonts is set.

keys.name: serif-font
keys.description: A value to be used to override serif desktop font when use-gnome-fonts is set.

keys.name: monospace-font
keys.description: A value to be used to override monospace desktop font when use-gnome-fonts is set.

keys.name: enable-user-css
keys.description: Use a custom CSS file to modify websites own CSS.

keys.name: enable-user-js
keys.description: Use a custom JS file to modify websites.

keys.name: enable-spell-checking
keys.description: Spell check any text typed in editable areas.

keys.name: default-encoding
keys.description: Default encoding. Accepted values are the ones WebKitGTK can understand.

keys.name: language
keys.description: Preferred languages. Array of locale codes or “system” to use current locale.

keys.name: enable-popups
keys.description: Allow sites to open new windows using JavaScript (if JavaScript is enabled).

keys.name: user-agent
keys.description: String that will be used as user agent, to identify the browser to the web servers.

keys.name: enable-adblock
keys.description: Whether to block the embedded advertisements that web pages might want to show.

keys.name: remember-passwords
keys.description: Whether to store and prefill passwords in websites.

keys.name: autofill-data
keys.description: Whether to auto fill form data in websites.

keys.name: enable-site-specific-quirks
keys.description: Enable quirks to make specific websites work better. You might want to disable this setting if debugging a specific issue.

keys.name: enable-itp
keys.description: Whether to enable Intelligent Tracking Prevention.

keys.name: enable-website-data-storage
keys.summary: Allow websites to store local website data
keys.description: Whether to allow websites to store cookies, local storage data, and IndexedDB databases. Disabling this will break many websites.

keys.name: default-zoom-level
keys.summary: Default zoom level for new pages

keys.name: reader-mode-zoom-level
keys.description: Sets the zoom level for reader mode pages.

keys.name: enable-autosearch
keys.description: Whether to automatically search the web when something that does not look like a URL is entered in the address bar. If this setting is disabled, everything will be loaded as a URL unless a search engine is explicitly selected from the dropdown menu.

keys.name: enable-mouse-gestures
keys.description: Whether to enable mouse gestures. Mouse gestures are based on Opera’s behaviour and are activated using the middle mouse button + gesture.

keys.name: enable-navigation-gestures
keys.description: Whether to enable back and forward navigation gestures.

keys.name: last-upload-directory
keys.description: Keep track of last upload directory

keys.name: last-download-directory
keys.description: Keep track of last download directory

keys.name: hardware-acceleration-policy
keys.description: Whether to enable hardware acceleration. Possible values are “always” and “never”. Hardware acceleration may be required to achieve acceptable performance on embedded devices, but increases memory usage requirements and could expose severe hardware-specific graphics driver bugs.

keys.name: ask-on-download
keys.description: Whether to present a directory chooser dialog for every download.

keys.name: switch-to-new-tab
keys.summary: Enable immediately switch to new open tab
keys.description: Whether to automatically switch to a new open tab.

keys.name: enable-webextensions
keys.description: Whether to enable WebExtensions. WebExtensions is a cross-browser system for extensions.

keys.name: webextensions-active
keys.description: Indicates which WebExtensions are set to active.

keys.name: show-developer-actions
keys.description: Whether to show developer context menu actions. This allows use of the Page Source and Inspect Element actions.

keys.name: always-show-full-url
keys.description: Whether to show the full URL in the address bar even when unfocused.

keys.name: auto-open-schemes
keys.description: This is a permission map of security origins to URL schemes.

keys.name: additional-urls
keys.description: The list of URLs that should be opened by the web application

keys.name: show-navigation-buttons
keys.description: Whether to show buttons for navigation in WebApp.

keys.name: run-in-background
keys.description: If enabled, application continues running in the background after closing the window.

keys.name: system
keys.description: If enabled, application cannot be edited or removed.

keys.name: download-dir
keys.description: The path of the folder where to download files to; or “Downloads” to use the default downloads folder, or “Desktop” to use the desktop folder.

keys.name: window-position
keys.description: The position to use for a new window that is not restored from a previous session.

keys.name: window-size
keys.description: The size to use for a new window that is not restored from a previous session.

keys.name: is-maximized
keys.description: Whether a new window that is not restored from a previous session should be initially maximized.

keys.name: disable-history
keys.description: If set to “true”, forward and back buttons are disabled, preventing users from accessing immediate browser history

keys.name: sync-token-server
keys.description: URL to a custom Firefox Sync token server.

keys.name: sync-accounts-server
keys.description: URL to a custom Firefox Sync accounts server.

keys.name: sync-user
keys.description: The email linked to the Mozilla account used to sync data with Mozilla’s servers.

keys.name: sync-time
keys.description: The UNIX time at which last sync was made in seconds.

keys.name: sync-device-id
keys.description: The sync device ID of the current device.

keys.name: sync-device-name
keys.description: The sync device name of the current device.

keys.name: sync-frequency
keys.description: The number of minutes between two consecutive syncs.

keys.name: sync-with-firefox
keys.description: TRUE if Ephy collections should be synced with Firefox collections, FALSE otherwise.

keys.name: sync-bookmarks-enabled
keys.description: TRUE if bookmarks collection should be synced, FALSE otherwise.

keys.name: sync-bookmarks-time
keys.description: The timestamp at which last bookmarks sync was made.

keys.name: sync-bookmarks-initial
keys.description: TRUE if bookmarks collection needs to be synced for the first time, FALSE otherwise.

keys.name: sync-passwords-enabled
keys.description: TRUE if passwords collection should be synced, FALSE otherwise.

keys.name: sync-passwords-time
keys.description: The timestamp at which last passwords sync was made.

keys.name: sync-passwords-initial
keys.description: TRUE if passwords collection needs to be synced for the first time, FALSE otherwise.

keys.name: sync-history-enabled
keys.description: TRUE if history collection should be synced, FALSE otherwise.

keys.name: sync-history-time
keys.description: The timestamp at which last history sync was made.

keys.name: sync-history-initial
keys.description: TRUE if history collection needs to be synced for the first time, FALSE otherwise.

keys.name: sync-open-tabs-enabled
keys.description: TRUE if open tabs collection should be synced, FALSE otherwise.

keys.name: sync-open-tabs-time
keys.description: The timestamp at which last open tabs sync was made.

keys.name: audio-device-permission
keys.summary: Decision to apply when microphone permission is requested for this host
keys.description: This option is used to save whether a given host has been given permission to access the user’s microphone. The “undecided” default means the browser needs to ask the user for permission, while “allow” and “deny” tell it to automatically make the decision upon request.

keys.name: geolocation-permission
keys.summary: Decision to apply when geolocation permission is requested for this host
keys.description: This option is used to save whether a given host has been given permission to access the user’s location. The “undecided” default means the browser needs to ask the user for permission, while “allow” and “deny” tell it to automatically make the decision upon request.

keys.name: notifications-permission
keys.summary: Decision to apply when notification permission is requested for this host
keys.description: This option is used to save whether a given host has been given permission to show notifications. The “undecided” default means the browser needs to ask the user for permission, while “allow” and “deny” tell it to automatically make the decision upon request.

keys.name: save-password-permission
keys.summary: Decision to apply when save password permission is requested for this host
keys.description: This option is used to save whether a given host has been given permission to save passwords. The “undecided” default means the browser needs to ask the user for permission, while “allow” and “deny” tell it to automatically make the decision upon request.

keys.name: video-device-permission
keys.summary: Decision to apply when webcam permission is requested for this host
keys.description: This option is used to save whether a given host has been given permission to access the user’s webcam. The “undecided” default means the browser needs to ask the user for permission, while “allow” and “deny” tell it to automatically make the decision upon request.

keys.name: display-device-permission
keys.summary: Decision to apply when display permission is requested for this host
keys.description: This option is used to save whether a given host has been given permission to access the user’s display. The “undecided” default means the browser needs to ask the user for permission, while “allow” and “deny” tell it to automatically make the decision upon request.

keys.name: advertisement-permission
keys.summary: Decision to apply when advertisement permission is requested for this host
keys.description: This option is used to save whether a given host has been given permission to allow advertisements. The “undecided” default means the browser global setting is used, while “allow” and “deny” tell it to automatically make the decision upon request.

keys.name: autoplay-permission
keys.summary: Decision to apply when an autoplay policy is requested for this host
keys.description: This option is used to save whether a given host has been given permission to autoplay. The “undecided” default means to allow autoplay of muted media, while “allow” and “deny” tell it to allow / deny all requests to autoplay media respectively.

keys.name: contacts-reminder-enabled
keys.description: Whether to set a reminder for birthdays and anniversaries

keys.name: contacts-reminder-interval
keys.description: Number of units for determining a birthday or anniversary reminder

keys.name: contacts-reminder-units
keys.description: Units for a birthday or anniversary reminder, “minutes”, “hours” or “days”

keys.name: defall-reminder-enabled
keys.summary: Default reminder for all events in chosen calendars
keys.description: Whether to show a specified reminder for all events in chosen calendars

keys.name: defall-reminder-interval
keys.summary: Interval of the default reminder for all events in chosen calendars
keys.description: Number of units for determining the reminder

keys.name: defall-reminder-units
keys.summary: Units of the default reminder for all events in chosen calendars
keys.description: Units for the default reminder for all events in chosen calendars, “minutes”, “hours” or “days”

keys.name: reminders-past-days
keys.summary: How many days the reminders-past can hold back. Reminders older than these days are automatically removed from the list of the past reminders. Use '0' to not remove old reminders.

keys.name: notify-programs
keys.description: Programs that are allowed to be run by reminders

keys.name: notify-enable-display
keys.description: When set to true, the desktop/system notifications are shown

keys.name: notify-enable-audio
keys.description: When set to true, the audio reminders will be played, otherwise audio reminders will be silently ignored

keys.name: notify-with-tray
keys.summary: Show reminders in notification tray only
keys.description: When set to true, the reminders are shown only in the notification tray, otherwise the reminders dialog is shown immediately

keys.name: notify-window-on-top
keys.summary: Show reminder notification dialog always on top
keys.description: Whether or not to show reminder notification dialog always on top. Note this works only as a hint for the window manager, which may or may not obey it.

keys.name: notify-window-x
keys.summary: X position of the reminder notification dialog

keys.name: notify-window-y
keys.summary: Y position of the reminder notification dialog

keys.name: notify-window-width
keys.summary: Width of the reminder notification dialog

keys.name: notify-window-height
keys.summary: Height of the reminder notification dialog

keys.name: notify-window-paned-position
keys.summary: Size in pixels of the event list in the reminder notification dialog

keys.name: notify-completed-tasks
keys.summary: Show reminder notification for completed tasks

keys.name: notify-past-events
keys.summary: Show reminder notification for past events

keys.name: notify-last-snooze-minutes
keys.summary: The last used snooze time, in minutes; negative number always means before start, otherwise the actual value is used in conjunction with “notify-last-snooze-from-start”

keys.name: notify-last-snooze-from-start
keys.summary: Whether the last snooze time is calculated relative to the event/task start; false to count relative to the current time

keys.name: notify-custom-snooze-times
keys.summary: User-defined snooze times, in minutes, encoded into string as a number, where zero or negative means “before start”. When the number is prefixed with a star, then it means after start, otherwise the time is relative to the current time, aka “snooze for N minutes”

keys.name: delete-meeting-on-decline
keys.summary: Delete meeting from calendar on Decline

keys.name: migrated
keys.summary: Whether the migration of old setting was already done

keys.name: camel-gpg-binary
keys.summary: An absolute path where the gpg (or gpg2) binary is
keys.description: An example is “/usr/bin/gpg”; when it is left empty, or an incorrect value is set, then it will be searched for. Change requires restart of the application.

keys.name: camel-cipher-load-photos
keys.summary: Whether to load photos of signers/encrypters
keys.description: When set to “true”, tries to load also photo of the signers/encrypters, if available in the key/certificate.

keys.name: camel-smtp-helo-argument
keys.description: When not empty, it's used as the SMTP HELO/EHLO argument, instead of the local host name/IP.

keys.name: camel-message-info-user-headers
keys.description: These headers can be stored in the folder summary, possibly being visible in the GUI. The value can contain a pipe character ('|'), which delimits the display name from the header name. Example: 'Spam Score|X-Spam-Score'

keys.name: network-monitor-gio-name
keys.summary: GIO name of the GNetworkMonitor to use for an ENetworkMonitor instance
keys.description: When set to an unknown value, then the default GNetworkMonitor is used in the background. A special value “always-online” is used for no network monitoring.

keys.name: autoconfig-directory
keys.summary: A full path to a directory where .source files with preconfigured options can be stored
keys.description: This directory, if filled with an existing path, is checked additionally to XDG configure directories.

keys.name: autoconfig-variables
keys.summary: A list of variables which can be part of the autoconfig .source files
keys.description: Each item of the array is expected to be of the form: name=value. These variables are checked before environment variables, but after the predefined USER, REALNAME and HOST variables.

keys.name: oauth2-services-hint
keys.summary: A list of hints for OAuth2 services
keys.description:
Users can extend the list of supported protocols and hostnames for defined OAuth2 services, in addition to those hard-coded.
      Each line can be of the form:
         servicename[-protocol]:hostname1,hostname2,...
      where “servicename” is the actual service name;
      the “-protocol” is optional, and if written, then the service can be used only if both “protocol” and “hostnameX” match;
      the “hostnameX” is the actual host name to compare with, case insensitively.
      Each line can contain multiple values, separated by comma. There can be provided multiple lines
      for one OAuth2 service. Note that the actual URL where the token is requested and refreshed cannot
      be changed here, the hostname is to allow other servers, where the OAuth2 service can be used.

      Examples:
         Company:mail.company.com — enables “Company” OAuth2 authentication for “mail.company.com” host
	 Company-CalDAV:caldav.company.com — enables “Company” OAuth2 authentication for any “CalDAV” source, which reads data from “caldav.company.com” host

keys.name: oauth2-google-client-id
keys.summary: An OAuth2 client ID to use to connect to Google servers, instead of the one provided during build time
keys.description: User-specified OAuth2 client ID for Google servers. Empty string means to use the one provided during build time. Change of this requires restart.

keys.name: oauth2-google-client-secret
keys.summary: An OAuth2 client secret to use to connect to Google servers, instead of the one provided during build time
keys.description: User-specified OAuth2 client secret for Google servers. Empty string means to use the one provided during build time. Change of this requires restart.

keys.name: oauth2-outlook-client-id
keys.summary: An OAuth2 client ID to use to connect to Outlook servers, instead of the one provided during build time
keys.description: User-specified OAuth2 client ID for Outlook servers. Empty string means to use the one provided during build time. Change of this requires restart.

keys.name: oauth2-outlook-client-secret
keys.summary: An OAuth2 client secret to use to connect to Outlook servers, instead of the one provided during build time
keys.description: User-specified OAuth2 client secret for Outlook servers. Empty string means to use the one provided during build time. Change of this requires restart.

keys.name: oauth2-yahoo-client-id
keys.summary: An OAuth2 client ID to use to connect to Yahoo! servers, instead of the one provided during build time
keys.description: User-specified OAuth2 client ID for Yahoo! servers. Empty string means to use the one provided during build time. Change of this requires restart.

keys.name: oauth2-yahoo-client-secret
keys.summary: An OAuth2 client secret to use to connect to Yahoo! servers, instead of the one provided during build time
keys.description: User-specified OAuth2 client secret for Yahoo! servers. Empty string means to use the one provided during build time. Change of this requires restart.

keys.name: limit-operations-in-power-saver-mode
keys.summary: Whether to limit operations in Power Saver mode
keys.description: When set to “true”, possibly expensive operations required to refresh books/calendars/mail accounts/... are skipped when the machine is in the Power Saver mode.

keys.name: start-offline
keys.description: Whether Evolution will start up in offline mode instead of online mode.

keys.name: proxy-type
keys.description: This key was deprecated in version 3.12 and should no longer be used.  Proxy settings are now integrated into Evolution-Data-Server’s account system.  See the ESourceProxy API documentation for details.

keys.name: use-http-proxy
keys.description: This key was deprecated in version 3.12 and should no longer be used.  Proxy settings are now integrated into Evolution-Data-Server’s account system.  See the ESourceProxy API documentation for details.

keys.name: use-authentication
keys.description: This key was deprecated in version 3.12 and should no longer be used.  Proxy settings are now integrated into Evolution-Data-Server’s account system.  See the ESourceProxy API documentation for details.

keys.name: http-host
keys.description: This key was deprecated in version 3.12 and should no longer be used.  Proxy settings are now integrated into Evolution-Data-Server’s account system.  See the ESourceProxy API documentation for details.

keys.name: http-port
keys.description: This key was deprecated in version 3.12 and should no longer be used.  Proxy settings are now integrated into Evolution-Data-Server’s account system.  See the ESourceProxy API documentation for details.

keys.name: authentication-user
keys.description: This key was deprecated in version 3.12 and should no longer be used.  Proxy settings are now integrated into Evolution-Data-Server’s account system.  See the ESourceProxy API documentation for details.

keys.name: authentication-password
keys.description: This key was deprecated in version 3.12 and should no longer be used.  Proxy settings are now integrated into Evolution-Data-Server’s account system.  See the ESourceProxy API documentation for details.

keys.name: ignore-hosts
keys.summary: (Deprecated) List of hosts to connect to without proxy
keys.description: This key was deprecated in version 3.12 and should no longer be used.  Proxy settings are now integrated into Evolution-Data-Server’s account system.  See the ESourceProxy API documentation for details.

keys.name: secure-host

keys.name: enable-fingerprint-authentication
keys.summary: Whether or not to allow fingerprint readers for login
keys.description:
The login screen can optionally allow users who have enrolled
        their fingerprints to log in using those prints.

keys.name: enable-smartcard-authentication
keys.summary: Whether or not to allow smartcard readers for login
keys.description:
The login screen can optionally allow users who have smartcards
        to log in using those smartcards.

keys.name: enable-password-authentication
keys.summary: Whether or not to allow passwords for login
keys.description:
The login screen can be configured to disallow password authentication,
        forcing the user to use smartcard or fingerprint authentication.

keys.name: logo
keys.summary: Path to small image at top of user list
keys.description:
The login screen can optionally show a small image to provide site
        administrators and distributions a way to display branding.

keys.name: fallback-logo
keys.summary: Path to small image at top of user list
keys.description:
The fallback login screen can optionally show a small image to provide
        site administrators and distributions a way to display branding.

keys.name: disable-user-list
keys.description:
The login screen normally shows a list of available users to log in
        as. This setting can be toggled to disable showing the user list.

keys.name: banner-message-enable
keys.description: Set to true to show the banner message text.

keys.name: banner-message-source
keys.description: The source of the text banner message on the login screen.

keys.name: banner-message-text
keys.description: Text banner message to show in the login window.

keys.name: banner-message-path
keys.description: Path to text file with banner message to show in the login window.

keys.name: disable-restart-buttons
keys.description: Set to true to disable showing the restart buttons in the login window.

keys.name: allowed-failures
keys.description:
The number of times a user is allowed to attempt authentication, before
        giving up and going back to user selection.

keys.name: overlay-key
keys.summary: Modifier to use for extended window management operations
keys.description:
This key will initiate the “overlay”, which is a combination window
        overview and application launching system.  The default is intended
        to be the “Windows key” on PC hardware.

        It’s expected that this binding either the default or set to
        the empty string.

keys.name: attach-modal-dialogs
keys.description:
When true, instead of having independent titlebars, modal dialogs
        appear attached to the titlebar of the parent window and are moved
        together with the parent window.

keys.name: edge-tiling
keys.summary: Enable edge tiling when dropping windows on screen edges
keys.description:
If enabled, dropping windows on vertical screen edges maximizes them
        vertically and resizes them horizontally to cover half of the available
        area. Dropping windows on the top screen edge maximizes them completely.

keys.name: dynamic-workspaces
keys.description:
Determines whether workspaces are managed dynamically or
        whether there’s a static number of workspaces (determined
        by the num-workspaces key in org.gnome.desktop.wm.preferences).

keys.name: workspaces-only-on-primary
keys.description:
Determines whether workspace switching should happen for windows
        on all monitors or only for windows on the primary monitor.

keys.name: focus-change-on-pointer-rest
keys.summary: Delay focus changes until the pointer stops moving
keys.description:
If set to true, and the focus mode is either “sloppy” or “mouse”
        then the focus will not be changed immediately when entering a
        window, but only after the pointer stops moving.

keys.name: draggable-border-width
keys.description:
The amount of total draggable borders. If the theme’s visible
          borders are not enough, invisible borders will be added to meet
          this value.

keys.name: auto-maximize
keys.summary: Auto maximize nearly monitor sized windows
keys.description:
If enabled, new windows that are initially the size of the monitor
        automatically get maximized.

keys.name: center-new-windows
keys.summary: Place new windows in the center
keys.description:
When true, the new windows will always be put in the center of the
        active screen of the monitor.

keys.name: experimental-features
keys.description:
To enable experimental features, add the feature keyword to the list.
        Whether the feature requires restarting the compositor depends on the
        given feature. Any experimental feature is not required to still be
        available, or configurable. Don’t expect adding anything in this
        setting to be future proof.

        Currently possible keywords:

        • “scale-monitor-framebuffer” — makes mutter default to layout logical
                                        monitors in a logical pixel coordinate
                                        space, while scaling monitor
                                        framebuffers instead of window content,
                                        to manage HiDPI monitors. Does not
                                        require a restart.

        • “kms-modifiers”             — makes mutter always allocate scanout
                                        buffers with explicit modifiers, if
                                        supported by the driver. Requires a
                                        restart.

        • “autoclose-xwayland”        — automatically terminates Xwayland if all
                                        relevant X11 clients are gone.
                                        Requires a restart.

        • “variable-refresh-rate”     — makes mutter dynamically adjust the
                                        refresh rate of the monitor when
                                        applicable if supported by the monitor,
                                        GPU and DRM driver. Configurable in
                                        Settings. Requires a restart.

        • “xwayland-native-scaling”   — lets Xwayland clients use their native
                                        scaling support. If scaling is not
                                        supported by client, the client will
                                        be unscaled. Setting only takes effect
                                        when “scale-monitor-framebuffer” is
                                        enabled as well.

keys.name: locate-pointer-key
keys.summary: Modifier to use to locate the pointer
keys.description: This key will initiate the “locate pointer” action.

keys.name: check-alive-timeout
keys.description:
Number of milliseconds a client has to respond to a ping request in
        order to not be detected as frozen. Using 0 will disable the alive check
        completely.

keys.name: output-luminance
keys.description:
Per output and color mode luminance setting. Each entry consists of a
        tuple with connector, vendor, product, serial, and a color mode, as well
        as an associated floating point value representing the output luminance
        in percent (%). The default when not specified is 100%.

keys.name: cancel-input-capture
keys.summary: Cancel any active input capture session

keys.name: xwayland-allow-grabs
keys.summary: Allow X11 grabs to lock keyboard focus with Xwayland
keys.description:
Allow all keyboard events to be routed to X11 “override redirect”
        windows with a grab when running in Xwayland.

        This option is to support X11 clients which map an “override redirect”
        window (which do not receive keyboard focus) and issue a keyboard
        grab to force all keyboard events to that window.

        This option is seldom used and has no effect on regular X11 windows
        which can receive keyboard focus under normal circumstances.

        For a X11 grab to be taken into account under Wayland, the client must
        also either send a specific X11 ClientMessage to the root window or be
        among the applications allowed in key “xwayland-grab-access-rules”.

keys.name: xwayland-grab-access-rules
keys.summary: Xwayland applications allowed to issue keyboard grabs
keys.description:
List the resource names or resource class of X11 windows either
        allowed or not allowed to issue X11 keyboard grabs under Xwayland.

        The resource name or resource class of a given X11 window can be
        obtained using the command “xprop WM_CLASS”.

        Wildcards “*” and jokers “?” in the values are supported.

        Values starting with “!” are denied, which has precedence over
        the list of values allowed, to revoke applications from the default
	system list.

        The default system list includes the following applications:

        “gnome-boxes,remote-viewer,virt-viewer,virt-manager,vinagre,vncviewer,Xephyr”

        Users can break an existing grab by using the specific keyboard
        shortcut defined by the keybinding key “restore-shortcuts”.

keys.name: xwayland-disable-extension
keys.summary: Disable selected X extensions in Xwayland
keys.description:
This option disables the selected X extensions in Xwayland if
        Xwayland was built with support for those X extensions.

        This option has no effect if Xwayland was built without support
        for the selected extensions.

        Xwayland needs to be restarted for this setting to take effect.

keys.name: xwayland-allow-byte-swapped-clients
keys.summary: Allow X11 clients with a different endianness to connect to Xwayland
keys.description:
Allow connections from clients with an endianness different to that
	of Xwayland.

	The X server byte-swapping code is a huge attack surface, much of
	that code in Xwayland is prone to security issues.

	The use-case of byte-swapped clients is very niche, and disabled by
	default in Xwayland.

	Enable this option to instruct Xwayland to accept connections from
	X11 clients with a different endianness.

        This option has no effect if Xwayland does not support the command
	line option +byteswappedclients/-byteswappedclients to control that
	setting.

        Xwayland needs to be restarted for this setting to take effect.

keys.name: always-use-location-entry
keys.summary: Always use the location entry, instead of the pathbar
keys.description: If set to true, Files will always use a textual input entry for the location toolbar, instead of the pathbar.

keys.name: recursive-search
keys.description: Locations in which Files should search subfolders. Available values are “local-only”, “always”, “never”.

keys.name: search-filter-time-type
keys.summary: Filter the search dates using either last used or last modified
keys.description: Filter the search dates using either last used or last modified.

keys.name: show-delete-permanently

keys.description: If set to true, Files will show a delete permanently context menu item to bypass the Trash.

keys.name: show-create-link
keys.summary: Whether to show context menu items to create links from copied or selected files
keys.description: If set to true, Files will show context menu items to create links from the copied or selected files.

keys.name: show-directory-item-counts
keys.summary: When to show number of items in a folder
keys.description: Speed tradeoff for when to show the number of items in a folder. If set to “always” then always show item counts, even if the folder is on a remote server. If set to “local-only” then only show counts for local file systems. If set to “never” then never bother to compute item counts.

keys.name: click-policy
keys.summary: Type of click used to launch/open files
keys.description: Possible values are “single” to launch files on a single click, or “double” to launch them on a double click.

keys.name: install-mime-activation
keys.summary: Show the package installer for unknown MIME types
keys.description: Whether to show the user a package installer dialog in case an unknown MIME type is opened, in order to search for an app to handle it.

keys.name: mouse-use-extra-buttons
keys.summary: Use extra mouse button events in Files
keys.description: For users with mice that have “Forward” and “Back” buttons, this key will determine if any action is taken inside of Files when either is pressed.

keys.name: mouse-forward-button
keys.summary: Mouse button to activate the “Forward” command in browser window
keys.description: For users with mice that have buttons for “Forward” and “Back”, this key will set which button activates the “Forward” command in a browser window. Possible values range between 6 and 14.

keys.name: mouse-back-button
keys.summary: Mouse button to activate the “Back” command in browser window
keys.description: For users with mice that have buttons for “Forward” and “Back”, this key will set which button activates the “Back” command in a browser window. Possible values range between 6 and 14.

keys.name: show-image-thumbnails
keys.summary: When to show thumbnails of files
keys.description: Speed trade-off for when to show a file as a thumbnail. If set to “always” then always thumbnail, even if the folder is on a remote server. If set to “local-only” then only show thumbnails for local file systems. If set to “never” then never bother to thumbnail files, just use a generic icon. Despite what the name may suggest, this applies to any previewable file type.

keys.name: thumbnail-limit
keys.description: Images over this size (in megabytes) won’t be thumbnailed. The purpose of this setting is to avoid thumbnailing large images that may take a long time to load or use lots of memory.

keys.name: default-sort-order
keys.description: The default sort-order for items in the icon view. Possible values are “name”, “size”, “type”, “mtime”, “atime” and “starred”.

keys.name: default-sort-in-reverse-order
keys.summary: Reverse sort order in new windows
keys.description: If true, files in new windows will be sorted in reverse order. I.e., if sorted by name, then instead of sorting the files from “a” to “z”, they will be sorted from “z” to “a”; if sorted by size, instead of being incrementally they will be sorted decrementally.

keys.name: default-folder-viewer
keys.description: When a folder is visited this viewer is used. Possible values are “list-view”, and “icon-view”.

keys.name: show-hidden-files

keys.name: migrated-gtk-settings

keys.name: open-folder-on-dnd-hover
