keys.description: If this is set to true, when performing a drag and drop operation the hovered folder will open automatically after a timeout.

keys.name: fts-enabled

keys.name: date-time-format
keys.summary: How to display file timestamps in the views
keys.description: If set to 'simple', Files will show Today and Yesterday with time, otherwise the exact date without time. If set to 'detailed', it will always show the exact date and time.

keys.name: default-compression-format

keys.name: left-handed

keys.description: Whether the command can be invoked while the lockscreen is shown

keys.name: idle-brightness
keys.summary: The brightness of the screen when idle
keys.description: This is the laptop panel screen brightness used when the session is idle.

keys.name: idle-dim
keys.summary: Dim the screen after a period of inactivity
keys.description: If the screen should be dimmed to save power when the computer is idle.

keys.name: sleep-inactive-ac-timeout
keys.summary: Sleep timeout computer when on AC
keys.description: The amount of time in seconds the computer on AC power needs to be inactive before it goes to sleep. A value of 0 means never.

keys.name: sleep-inactive-ac-type
keys.summary: Whether to hibernate, suspend or do nothing when inactive
keys.description: The type of sleeping that should be performed when the computer is inactive.

keys.name: sleep-inactive-battery-timeout
keys.summary: Sleep timeout computer when on battery
keys.description: The amount of time in seconds the computer on battery power needs to be inactive before it goes to sleep. A value of 0 means never.

keys.name: sleep-inactive-battery-type
keys.summary: Whether to hibernate, suspend or do nothing when inactive
keys.description: The type of sleeping that should be performed when the computer is inactive.

keys.name: ambient-enabled
keys.description: If the ambient light sensor functionality is enabled.

keys.name: power-button-action
keys.description: The action to take when the system power button is pressed. Virtual machines only honor the 'nothing' action, and will shutdown otherwise. Tablets always suspend, ignoring all the other action options.

keys.name: power-saver-profile-on-low-battery
keys.summary: Enable power-saver profile when battery is low
keys.description: Automatically enable the "power-saver" profile using power-profiles-daemon if the battery is low.

keys.name: enabled-connections
keys.summary: On which connections the service is enabled
keys.description: The list of NetworkManager connections (each one represented with its UUID) on which this service is enabled and started.

keys.name: unlock-sim
keys.description: Unlock any sim cards right away.

keys.name: disabled-gtk-modules
keys.summary: List of explicitly disabled GTK+ modules
keys.description: A list of strings representing the GTK+ modules that will not be loaded, even if enabled by default in their configuration.

keys.name: enabled-gtk-modules
keys.summary: List of explicitly enabled GTK+ modules
keys.description: A list of strings representing the GTK+ modules that will be loaded, usually in addition to conditional and forcibly disabled ones.

keys.name: overrides
keys.summary: A dictionary of XSETTINGS to override
keys.description: This dictionary maps XSETTINGS names to overrides values. The values must be either strings, signed int32s or (in the case of colors), 4-tuples of uint16 (red, green, blue, alpha; 65535 is fully opaque).

keys.name: antialiasing
keys.description: The type of antialiasing to use when rendering fonts. Possible values are: “none” for no antialiasing, “grayscale” for standard grayscale antialiasing, and “rgba” for subpixel antialiasing (LCD screens only).

keys.name: hinting
keys.description: The type of hinting to use when rendering fonts. Possible values are: “none” for no hinting and “slight” for fitting only to the Y-axis like Microsoft’s ClearType, DirectWrite and Adobe’s proprietary font rendering engine. Ignores native hinting within the font, generates hints algorithmically. Used on Ubuntu by default. Recommended. The meaning of “medium” and “full” depends on the font format (.ttf, .otf, .pfa/.pfb) and the installed version of FreeType. They usually try to fit glyphs to both the X and the Y axis (except for .otf: Y-only). This can lead to distortion and/or inconsistent rendering depending on the quality of the font, the font format and the state of FreeType’s font engines.

keys.name: rgba-order
keys.description: The order of subpixel elements on an LCD screen; only used when antialiasing is set to “rgba”. Possible values are: “rgb” for red on left (most common), “bgr” for blue on left, “vrgb” for red on top, “vbgr” for red on bottom.

keys.name: apps-menu-toggle-menu
keys.summary: Keybinding to open the applications menu
keys.description: Keybinding to open the applications menu.

keys.name: grouping-mode
keys.description:
Decides when to group windows from the same application on the
        window list. Possible values are “never”, “auto” and “always”.

keys.name: display-all-workspaces
keys.description: Whether to show windows from all workspaces or only the current one.

keys.name: show-on-all-monitors
keys.summary: Show the window list on all monitors
keys.description:
Whether to show the window list on all connected monitors or
        only on the primary one.

keys.name: embed-previews
keys.summary: Show workspace previews in window list

keys.name: development-tools
keys.summary: Enable internal tools useful for developers and testers from Alt-F2
keys.description:
Allows access to internal debugging and monitoring tools
        using the Alt-F2 dialog.

keys.name: enabled-extensions
keys.description:
GNOME Shell extensions have a UUID property; this key lists extensions
        which should be loaded. Any extension that wants to be loaded needs
        to be in this list. You can also manipulate this list with the
        EnableExtension and DisableExtension D-Bus methods on org.gnome.Shell.

keys.name: disabled-extensions
keys.summary: UUIDs of extensions to force disabling
keys.description:
GNOME Shell extensions have a UUID property; this key lists extensions
        which should be disabled, even if loaded as part of the current mode.
        You can also manipulate this list with the EnableExtension and
        DisableExtension D-Bus methods on org.gnome.Shell.
        This key takes precedence over the “enabled-extensions” setting.

keys.name: disable-user-extensions
keys.description:
Disable all extensions the user has enabled without affecting
        the “enabled-extension” setting.

keys.name: allow-extension-installation
keys.description:
Allow users to install extensions in their home folder. If disabled,
        the InstallRemoteExtension D-Bus method will fail, and extensions
        are only loaded from system directories on startup.
        It does not affect extensions that are already loaded, so a change
        only takes full effect on the next login.

keys.name: disable-extension-version-validation
keys.summary: Disables the validation of extension version compatibility
keys.description:
GNOME Shell will only load extensions that claim to support the current
        running version. Enabling this option will disable this check and try to
        load all extensions regardless of the versions they claim to support.

keys.name: favorite-apps
keys.summary: List of desktop file IDs for favorite applications
keys.description:
The applications corresponding to these identifiers
        will be displayed in the favorites area.

keys.name: looking-glass-history
keys.summary: History for the looking glass dialog

keys.name: always-show-log-out
keys.summary: Always show the “Log Out” action in the system menu
keys.description:
This key overrides the automatic hiding of the “Log out” action in the
        system menu for logged-in situations where there is a single, local,
        non-system user and only a single available session type (e.g. GNOME on
        Wayland).

keys.name: remember-mount-password
keys.summary: Whether to remember password for mounting encrypted or remote filesystems
keys.description:
The shell will request a password when an encrypted device or a
        remote filesystem is mounted.  If the password can be saved for
        future use a “Remember Password” checkbox will be present.
        This key sets the default state of the checkbox.

keys.name: last-selected-power-profile
keys.summary: The last selected non-default power profile
keys.description:
Some systems support more than two power profiles. In order to still support
        toggling between two profiles, this key records the last selected non-default
        profile.

keys.name: welcome-dialog-last-shown-version
keys.summary: The last version the “Welcome to GNOME” dialog was shown for
keys.description:
This key determines for which version the “Welcome to GNOME” dialog was
        last shown. An empty string represents the oldest possible version, and
        a huge number will represent versions that do not exist yet. This huge
        number can be used to effectively disable the dialog.

keys.name: app-picker-layout
keys.description:
Layout of the app picker. Each entry in the array is a page. Pages are
        stored in the order they appear in GNOME Shell. Each page contains an
        “application id” → 'data' pair. Currently, the following values are
        stored as 'data':
          • “position”: the position of the application icon in the page

keys.name: current-workspace-only
keys.description:
If true, only applications that have windows on the current workspace are shown in the switcher.
	Otherwise, all applications are included.

keys.name: app-icon-mode
keys.description:
Configures how the windows are shown in the switcher. Valid possibilities
	are “thumbnail-only” (shows a thumbnail of the window), “app-icon-only”
	(shows only the application icon) or “both”.

keys.name: current-workspace-only
keys.description:
If true, only windows from the current workspace are shown in the switcher.
	Otherwise, all windows are included.

keys.name: locations
keys.description: The locations to show in world clocks

keys.name: automatic-location
keys.description: Whether to fetch the current location or not

keys.name: locations
keys.description: The location for which to show a forecast

keys.name: compatible-projects
keys.description: This is a list of compatible projects we should show such as GNOME, KDE and XFCE.

keys.name: allow-updates
keys.summary: Whether to manage updates and upgrades in GNOME Software
keys.description: If disabled, GNOME Software will hide the updates panel, not perform any automatic updates actions or prompt for upgrades.

keys.name: download-updates
keys.description: If enabled, GNOME Software automatically downloads software updates in the background, also installing ones that do not require a reboot.

keys.name: download-updates-notify
keys.summary: Notify the user about software updated in the background
keys.description: If enabled, GNOME Software notifies the user about updates that happened whilst the user was idle.

keys.name: refresh-when-metered
keys.summary: Whether to automatically refresh when on a metered connection
keys.description: If enabled, GNOME Software automatically refreshes in the background even when using a metered connection (eventually downloading some metadata, checking for updates, etc., which may incur in costs for the user).

keys.name: first-run
keys.summary: Whether it’s the very first run of GNOME Software

keys.name: show-ratings
keys.summary: Show star ratings next to applications

keys.name: filter-default-branch
keys.summary: Filter applications based on the default branch set for the remote

keys.name: prompt-for-nonfree
keys.summary: Non-free applications show a warning dialog before install
keys.description: When non-free applications are installed a warning dialog can be shown. This controls if that dialog is suppressed.

keys.name: security-timestamp
keys.summary: The timestamp of the first security update, cleared after update

keys.name: screenshot-cache-age-maximum
keys.summary: The age in seconds to verify the upstream screenshot is still valid
keys.description:
Choosing a larger value will mean less round-trips to the remote server
        but updates to the screenshots may take longer to show to the user.
        A value of 0 means to never check the server if the image already exists
        in the cache.

keys.name: review-server
keys.summary: The server to use for application reviews

keys.name: review-karma-required
keys.summary: The minimum karma score for reviews
keys.description: Reviews with karma less than this number will not be shown.

keys.name: official-repos
keys.summary: A list of official repositories that should not be considered 3rd party

keys.name: required-repos
keys.summary: A list of required repositories that cannot be disabled or removed

keys.name: free-repos
keys.summary: A list of official repositories that should be considered free software

keys.name: free-repos-url
keys.summary: The licence URL to use when an application should be considered free software

keys.name: install-bundles-system-wide
keys.summary: Install bundled applications for all users on the system where possible

keys.name: enable-repos-dialog
keys.summary: Allow access to the Software Repositories dialog

keys.name: show-nonfree-ui
keys.summary: Show some UI elements informing the user that an app is non-free

keys.name: installed-page-show-size
keys.summary: Show the installed size for apps in the list of installed applications

keys.name: nonfree-software-uri
keys.summary: The URI that explains nonfree and proprietary software

keys.name: external-appstream-urls
keys.summary: A list of URLs pointing to appstream files that will be downloaded into an swcatalog folder

keys.name: external-appstream-system-wide
keys.summary: Install the AppStream files to a system-wide location for all users. If false, files are installed in non-standard $XDG_DATA_HOME/swcatalog/xml directory

keys.name: packaging-format-preference
keys.summary: Priority order of packaging formats to prefer, with more important formats listed first. An empty array means the default order. Omitted formats are assumed to be listed last. Example packaging formats are: deb, flatpak, rpm, snap. The formats can be optionally specified with an origin name, divided by a colon, for example 'flatpak:flathub'.

keys.name: flatpak-purge-timestamp
keys.summary: The timestamp of the last attempt to remove unused Flatpak runtimes

keys.name: show-only-free-apps
keys.summary: Set to 'true' to show only freely licensed apps and hide any proprietary apps.

keys.name: show-only-verified-apps
keys.summary: Set to 'true' to show only apps from developers whose identity has been verified.

keys.name: packagekit-historical-updates-timestamp
keys.summary: The timestamp of the last received historical updates.

keys.name: account-id
keys.summary: A string storing the gnome-online-account id used to login

keys.name: display-local
keys.summary: How to display local DNS-SD services
keys.description: How DNS-SD services are displayed in the "network:///" location. Possible values are "merged", "separate" (the Local Network folder) and "disabled".

keys.name: extra-domains
keys.summary: Extra domains to look for DNS-SD services in
keys.description: Comma separated list of DNS-SD domains that should be visible in the "network:///" location.

keys.name: region
keys.summary: The locale to use for dates / numbers formats
keys.description: Specify the locale to be used for displaying dates, times and numbers formats.

keys.name: enabled
keys.description: If true, applications are allowed to access location information.

keys.name: max-accuracy-level
keys.summary: The maximum accuracy level of location.
keys.description:
Configures the maximum level of location accuracy applications are
        allowed to see. Valid options are “country”, “city”, “neighborhood”,
        “street”, and “exact” (typically requires GPS receiver). Please keep in
        mind that this only controls what GeoClue will allow applications to see
        and they can find user’s location on their own using network resources
        (albeit with street-level accuracy at best).

keys.name: mode
keys.description:
Select the proxy configuration mode. Supported values are “none”,
        “manual”, “auto”.

        If this is “none”, then proxies are not used.

        If it is “auto”, the autoconfiguration URL described by the
        “autoconfig-url” key is used.

        If it is “manual”, then the proxies described by
        “/system/proxy/http”, “/system/proxy/https”,
        “/system/proxy/ftp” and “/system/proxy/socks” will be used.
        Each of the 4 proxy types is enabled if its “host” key is
        non-empty and its “port” key is non-0.

        If an http proxy is configured, but an https proxy is not,
        then the http proxy is also used for https.

        If a SOCKS proxy is configured, it is used for all protocols,
        except that the http, https, and ftp proxy settings override
        it for those protocols only.

keys.name: autoconfig-url
keys.description:
URL that provides proxy configuration values. When mode is
        “auto”, this URL is used to look up proxy information for all
        protocols.

keys.name: ignore-hosts
keys.description:
This key contains a list of hosts which are connected to directly,
        rather than via the proxy (if it is active). The values can be
        hostnames, domains (using an initial wildcard like *.foo.com), IP host
        addresses (both IPv4 and IPv6) and network addresses with a netmask
        (something like 192.168.0.0/24).

keys.name: use-same-proxy
keys.summary: Use HTTP proxy for all protocols
keys.description: Whether to use the HTTP proxy for all protocols or not.

keys.name: enabled
keys.description:
This key is not used; HTTP proxying is enabled when the host
        key is non-empty and the port is non-0.

keys.name: host
keys.description: The machine name to proxy HTTP through.

keys.name: port
keys.description:
The port on the machine defined by “/system/proxy/http/host” that you
        proxy through.

keys.name: use-authentication
keys.description:
If true, then connections to the proxy server require authentication.
        The username/password combo is defined by
        “/system/proxy/http/authentication-user” and
        “/system/proxy/http/authentication-password”.

        This applies only to the http proxy; when using a separate
        https proxy, there is currently no way to specify that it
        should use authentication.

keys.name: authentication-user
keys.description: User name to pass as authentication when doing HTTP proxying.

keys.name: authentication-password
keys.description: Password to pass as authentication when doing HTTP proxying.

keys.name: host
keys.description: The machine name to proxy secure HTTP through.

keys.name: port
keys.description:
The port on the machine defined by “/system/proxy/https/host” that you
        proxy through.

keys.name: host
keys.description: The machine name to proxy FTP through.

keys.name: port
keys.description:
The port on the machine defined by “/system/proxy/ftp/host” that you
        proxy through.

keys.name: host
keys.description: The machine name to use as a SOCKS proxy.

keys.name: port
keys.description:
The port on the machine defined by “/system/proxy/socks/host” that you
        proxy through.

keys.name: display-mode
keys.description: How SMB devices are displayed in the "network:///" location. Possible values are "merged", "separate" (the Windows Network folder) and "disabled".

keys.name: workgroup
keys.description: The Windows networking workgroup or domain that the user is part of. In order for a new workgroup to fully take effect the user may need to log out and log back in.

keys.name: display-mode
keys.description: How WSDD devices are displayed in the "network:///" location. Possible values are "merged", "separate" (the WSDD Network folder) and "disabled".

keys.name: custom-colors
keys.description:
An array of custom colors to show in the color chooser. Each color is
        specified as a tuple of four doubles, specifying RGBA values between
        0 and 1.

keys.name: selected-color
keys.description:
The selected color, described as a tuple whose first member is a
         boolean that is true if a color was selected, and the remaining
         four members are four doubles, specifying RGBA values between
         0 and 1.

keys.name: enable-inspector-keybinding
keys.description:
If this setting is true, GTK+ lets the user open an interactive
        debugging window with a keybinding. The default shortcuts for
        the keybinding are Control-Shift-I and Control-Shift-D.

keys.name: inspector-warning
keys.description:
If this setting is true, GTK+ shows a warning before letting
        the user use the interactive debugger.

keys.name: recent-emoji
keys.description:
An array of Emoji definitions to show in the Emoji chooser. Each Emoji is
        specified as an array of codepoints, name and shortname. The extra integer after this
        pair is the code of the Fitzpatrick modifier to use in place of a 0 in the
        codepoint array.

keys.name: location-mode
keys.description:
Controls whether the file chooser shows just a path bar, or a visible entry
        for the filename as well, for the benefit of typing-oriented users. The
        possible values for these modes are "path-bar" and "filename-entry".

keys.name: show-hidden
keys.description: Controls whether the file chooser shows hidden files or not.

keys.name: sort-directories-first
keys.description: If set to true, then folders are shown before files in the list.

keys.name: expand-folders
keys.description: This key is deprecated; do not use it.

keys.name: show-size-column
keys.description: Controls whether the file chooser shows a column with file sizes.

keys.name: show-type-column
keys.description: Controls whether the file chooser shows a column with file types.

keys.name: sort-column
keys.description:
Can be one of "name", "modified", or "size".  It controls
	which of the columns in the file chooser is used for sorting
	the list of files.

keys.name: sort-order
keys.description: Can be one of the strings "ascending" or "descending".

keys.name: window-position
keys.description:
The (x, y) coordinates of the upper-left corner of the GtkFileChooserDialog's
        window.

keys.name: window-size
keys.description: The size (width, height) of the GtkFileChooserDialog's window, in pixels.

keys.name: startup-mode
keys.description:
Either "recent" or "cwd"; controls whether the file chooser
	starts up showing the list of recently-used files, or the
	contents of the current working directory.

keys.name: sidebar-width
keys.description: Width in pixels of the file chooser's places sidebar.

keys.name: clock-format
keys.description: Whether the time is shown in 24h or 12h format.

keys.name: date-format
keys.description: The amount of detail to show in the Modified column.

keys.name: type-format
keys.description:
Different ways to show the 'Type' column information.
        Example outputs for a video mp4 file:
        'mime' -> 'video/mp4'
        'description' -> 'MPEG-4 video'
        'category' -> 'Video'

keys.name: font
keys.description: The font to be used for content.

keys.name: transition
keys.description: The transition to use when switching tabs.

keys.name: show-words
keys.description: Whether to show a word list in the sidebar

keys.name: debug-nodes
keys.description:
If this setting is true, the recorder will insert debug nodes
        into the recording.

keys.name: record-events
keys.description:
If this setting is true, the recorder will include events
        in the recording.

keys.name: highlight-sequences
keys.description:
If this setting is true, the recorder will highlight events
        that are part of an event sequence.

keys.name: dark
keys.description:
If this setting is true, the recorder will display render nodes
        on a dark background.

keys.name: recently-used-emoji
